Reply with only the number."},"uuid":"bb31542d-7829-4657-8f77-7c2115748236","timestamp":"2026-06-05T18:27:51.575Z","permissionMode":"default","promptSource":"typed","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/unnatigoel/FORGE_SPRINT_01","sessionId":"12cb72c7-8995-40c9-af85-285f1280b28e","version":"2.1.165","gitBranch":"HEAD"}
{"parentUuid":"bb31542d-7829-4657-8f77-7c2115748236","isSidechain":false,"attachment":{"type":"skill_listing","content":"- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., \"what car to buy\" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.\n- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors (\"from now on when X\", \"each time X\", \"whenever X\", \"before/after X\") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions (\"allow X\", \"add permission\", \"move permission to\"), env vars (\"set X=Y\"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: \"allow npm commands\", \"add bq permission to global settings\", \"move permission to user settings\", \"set DEBUG=true\", \"when claude stops show X\". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.\n- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: \"rebind ctrl+s\", \"add a chord shortcut\", \"change the submit key\", \"customize keybindings\".\n- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.\n- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.\n- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.\n- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.\n- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. \"check the deploy every 5 minutes\", \"keep running /babysit-prs\"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.\n- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.\nTRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it \"looks like a one-liner\" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).\nSKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).\n- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).\n- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation\n- review: Review a pull request\n- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch","skillCount":13,"isInitial":true,"names":["deep-research","update-config","keybindings-help","verify","code-review","simplify","fewer-permission-prompts","loop","claude-api","run","init","review","security-review"]},"type":"attachment","uuid":"900fb7b9-770e-44fe-a6e2-a3e42c3178b6","timestamp":"2026-06-05T18:27:51.575Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/unnatigoel/FORGE_SPRINT_01","sessionId":"12cb72c7-8995-40c9-af85-285f1280b28e","version":"2.1.165","gitBranch":"HEAD"}

If a suggested fix looks wrong for my setup, say so instead of running it.\n\n- Settings (/Users/unnatigoel/FORGE_SPRINT_01/.claude/settings.json › hooks.SessionStart.0.hooks): Expected array, but received undefined\n  Suggested fix: Hooks use a matcher + hooks array. {"parentUuid":"d919e36d-0d62-4239-acc6-5e2bd627dcea","isSidechain":false,"attachment":{"type":"skill_listing","content":"- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., \"what car to buy\" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.\n- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors (\"from now on when X\", \"each time X\", \"whenever X\", \"before/after X\") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions (\"allow X\", \"add permission\", \"move permission to\"), env vars (\"set X=Y\"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: \"allow npm commands\", \"add bq permission to global settings\", \"move permission to user settings\", \"set DEBUG=true\", \"when claude stops show X\". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.\n- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: \"rebind ctrl+s\", \"add a chord shortcut\", \"change the submit key\", \"customize keybindings\".\n- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.\n- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.\n- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.\n- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.\n- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. \"check the deploy every 5 minutes\", \"keep running /babysit-prs\"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.\n- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.\nTRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it \"looks like a one-liner\" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).\nSKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).\n- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).\n- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation\n- review: Review a pull request\n- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch","skillCount":13,"isInitial":true,"names":["deep-research","update-config","keybindings-help","verify","code-review","simplify","fewer-permission-prompts","loop","claude-api","run","init","review","security-review"]},"type":"attachment","uuid":"bd07a9ad-d80d-4820-8b5b-dcb3e6c59286","timestamp":"2026-06-06T08:37:21.438Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/unnatigoel/FORGE_SPRINT_01","sessionId":"42a3f8df-bf48-4047-9e41-7e0c0216a5bd","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"main"}

According to your doctor reports, every hook entry should follow this pattern:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"matcher\": \"...\", \n  \"hooks\": [...]\n}\n```\n\nBut instead you have direct arrays like `[{\"type\":\"command\"...}]`. This is a schema mismatch with what the settings validator expects.\n\n## What the fix will do\n\nI'll update `.claude/settings.json` to add:\n1. {"parentUuid":"9619a0fc-e532-4aee-8e65-5e8264e39ebb","isSidechain":false,"message":{"id":"msg_16e79b75543a24b315d7b8e8","type":"message","role":"assistant","model":"qwen3.5:9b","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"call_ehsy86ec","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/Users/unnatigoel/FORGE_SPRINT_01/validate_report.py","content":"#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"Validate outputs/report.json against required schema.\"\"\"\nimport json\n\n\ndef validate():\n    \"\"\"Validate report.json and return (valid, message).\"\"\"\n    try:\n        with open(\"outputs/report.json\", \"r\") as f:\n            data = json.load(f)\n    except FileNotFoundError:\n        return False, \"File not found: outputs/report.json\"\n    except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n        return False, f\"Invalid JSON: {e}\"\n\n    # 1. Top-level required fields exist with correct types\n    if not isinstance(data.get(\"site\"), str):\n        return False, \"'site' must be a string (got: \" + type(data.get(\"site\")).__name__)\n    if not isinstance(data.get(\"urls_crawled\"), int):\n        return False, \"'urls_crawled' must be an integer\"\n    if not isinstance(data.get(\"summary\"), dict):\n        return False, \"'summary' must be an object (dict)\"\n    if not isinstance(data.get(\"issues\"), list):\n        return False, \"'issues' must be a list\"\n    if not isinstance(data.get(\"run_meta\"), dict):\n        return False, \"'run_meta' must be an object (dict)\"\n\n    # 2. summary has required fields with correct types\n    if \"total_issues\" not in data[\"summary\"]:\n        return False, \"'summary.total_issues' is missing or incorrect type (expected int)\"\n    elif not isinstance(data[\"summary\"][\"total_issues\"], int):\n        return False, \"'summary.total_issues' must be an integer\"\n\n    by_severity = data.get(\"by_severity\", {})\n    if not isinstance(by_severity, dict):\n        return False, \"'summary.by_severity' must be a dictionary (got: \" + type(by_severity).__name__)\n    for sev in [\"High\", \"Medium\", \"Low\"]:\n        v = by_severity.get(sev)\n        if v is None or not isinstance(v, int):\n            return False, f\"'summary.by_severity.{sev}' must be an integer (got: {type(v).__name__ if v else 'missing'})\"\n\n    # 3. Every issue has required fields with correct types and valid severity values\n    allowed_severities = {\"High\", \"Medium\", \"Low\"}\n    for i, issue in enumerate(data[\"issues\"], start=1):\n        if not isinstance(issue.get(\"type\"), str):\n            return False, f\"issue[{i}].type must be a string (got: {type(issue.get('type')).__name__})\"\n        if issue.get(\"severity\") not in allowed_severities:\n            sev = issue.get(\"severity\", \"unknown\")\n            return False, f\"issue[{i}].severity '{sev}' is invalid (must be one of High/Medium/Low)\"\n        if not isinstance(issue.get(\"affected_urls\"), list):\n            return f\"issue[{i}].affected_urls must be a list (got: {type(issue['affected_urls']).__name__})\"\n        elif len(issue[\"affected_urls\"]) == 0 and \"URLs\" in issue.get(\"type\", \"\"):\n            # Allow empty for non-URL issues, but warn if type indicates URLs expected\n            pass\n        else:\n            affected = data[i].get('affected')\n"}}],"usage":{"input_tokens":24614,"cache_creation_input_tokens":0,"cache_read_input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":958,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"},"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_details":null},"type":"assistant","uuid":"b094ff26-d4a9-4d34-8245-4769e378335f","timestamp":"2026-06-06T08:46:25.569Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/unnatigoel/FORGE_SPRINT_01","sessionId":"42a3f8df-bf48-4047-9e41-7e0c0216a5bd","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"main"}

{"parentUuid":"ae191da8-cab2-4ecb-a3bb-76f3f69dbb18","isSidechain":false,"message":{"id":"msg_bd1c264f6681a75ccbd72f71","type":"message","role":"assistant","model":"qwen3.5:9b","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"call_5p73ls9v","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/Users/unnatigoel/FORGE_SPRINT_01/validate_report.py","content":"#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"Validate outputs/report.json against required schema.\"\"\"\nimport json\n\n\ndef validate():\n    \"\"\"Validate report.json and return (valid, message).\"\"\"\n    try:\n        with open(\"outputs/report.json\", \"r\") as f:\n            data = json.load(f)\n    except FileNotFoundError:\n        return False, \"FAIL - File not found: outputs/report.json\"\n    except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n        return False, f\"FAIL - Invalid JSON: {e}\"\n\n    # 1. Top-level required fields exist with correct types\n    site = data.get(\"site\")\n    if not isinstance(site, str):\n        msg = \"'site' must be a string (got:\" + type(site).__name__ + \")\" if site is not None else \"Missing 'site'\"\n        return False, f\"FAIL - {msg}\"\n\n    urls_crawled = data.get(\"urls_crawled\")\n    if not isinstance(urls_crawled, int):\n        msg = \"'urls_crawled' must be an integer (got:\" + type(urls_crawled).__name__ + \")\" if urls_crawled is not None else \"Missing 'urls_crawled'\"\n        return False, f\"FAIL - {msg}\"\n\n    summary = data.get(\"summary\")\n    if not isinstance(summary, dict):\n        msg = \"'summary' must be an object (dict)\" if type(summary).__name__ != \"NoneType\" else \"Missing 'summary'\"\n        return False, f\"FAIL - {msg}\"\n\n    issues_list = data.get(\"issues\", [])\n    if not isinstance(issues_list, list):\n        msg = \"'issues' must be a list (got:\" + type(issues_list).__name__ + \")\" if issues_list is not None else \"Missing 'issues'\"\n        return False, f\"FAIL - {msg}\"\n\n    run_meta = data.get(\"run_meta\")\n    if not isinstance(run_meta, dict):\n        msg = \"'run_meta' must be an object (dict)\" if type(run_meta).__name__ != \"NoneType\" else \"Missing 'run_meta'\"\n        return False, f\"FAIL - {msg}\"\n\n    # 2. summary has required fields with correct types\n    total_issues_val = summary.get(\"total_issues\")\n    if not isinstance(total_issues_val, int):\n        val_type = type(total_issues_val).__name__ if total_issues_val is not None else \"missing\"\n        return False, f\"FAIL - 'summary.total_issues' must be an integer (got: {val_type})\"\n\n    by_severity = summary.get(\"by_severity\", {})\n    if not isinstance(by_severity, dict):\n        msg = \"'summary.by_severity' must be a dictionary\" if type(by_severity).__name__ != \"NoneType\" else \"Missing 'by_severity'\"\n        return False, f\"FAIL - {msg}\"\n\n    severity_checks = [\"High\", \"Medium\", \"Low\"]\n    for sev in severity_checks:\n        v = by_severity.get(sev)\n        if not isinstance(v, int):\n            val_type = type(v).__name__ if v is not None else \"missing\"\n            return False, f\"FAIL - 'summary.by_severity.{sev}' must be an integer (got: {val_type})\"\n\n    # 3. Every issue has required fields with correct types and valid severity values\n    allowed_severities = {\"High\", \"Medium\", \"Low\"}\n    for i, issue in enumerate(issues_list, start=1):\n        if not isinstance(issue.get(\"type\"), str):\n            tname = type(issue.get(\"type\")).__name__ if issue.get(\"type\") is not None else \"missing\"\n            return False, f\"FAIL - issues[{i}].type must be a string (got: {tname})\"\n\n        sev_val = issue.get(\"severity\", \"\")\n        if sev_val not in allowed_severities:\n            return False, f\"FAIL - issues[{i}].severity '{sev_val}' is invalid (must be one of High/Medium/Low)\"\n\n        affected_urls = issue.get(\"affected_urls\")\n        if not isinstance(affected_urls, list):\n            tname = type(affected_urls).__name__ if affected_urls is not None else \"missing\"\n            return False, f\"FAIL - issues[{i}].affected_urls must be a list (got: {tname})\"\n\n        count_val = issue.get(\"count\")\n        if not isinstance(count_val, int):\n            tname = type(count_val).__name__ if count_val is not None else \"missing\"\n            return False, f\"FAIL - issues[{i}].count must be an integer (got: {tname})\"\n\n    # 4. run_meta has required fields with correct types\n    model_calls = run_meta.get(\"model_calls\")\n    if not isinstance(model_calls, int):\n        tname = type(model_calls).__name__ if model_calls is not None else \"missing\"\n        return False, f\"FAIL - 'run_meta.model_calls' must be an integer (got: {tname})\"\n\n    # 5. If fixes key exists: check its structure\n    fixes = data.get(\"fixes\")\n    if fixes is not None and not isinstance(fixes, dict):\n        return False, \"FAIL - 'fixes' object found but must be a dictionary (got:\" + type(fixes).__name__ + \")\"\n\n    if fixes:  # Only check contents if key exists\n        titles_list = fixes.get(\"titles\")\n        redirect_map = fixes.get(\"redirect_map\")\n\n        if not isinstance(titles_list, list):\n            return False, \"FAIL - 'fixes.titles' must be a list (got:\" + type(fixes.get('titles')).__name__ + \")\"\n\n        # Check that each title entry is a dict with required keys\n        for j, title_entry in enumerate(titles_list or [], start=1):\n            if not isinstance(title_entry, dict) and \"title\" in str(type(title_entry)):\n                return False, f\"FAIL - 'fixes.titles[{j}]' must be an object (got:\" + type(title_entry).__name__ + \")\"\n\n        if redirect_map is None:\n            # Optional but recommended; don't fail if absent unless explicitly required\n            pass  # Allow redirects to be optional per rulebook design\n\n    return True, \"PASS - report.json schema validation successful\"\n\n\ndef print_results(validated):\n    \"\"\"Print summary and detailed info.\"\"\"\n    with open(\"outputs/report.json\", \"r\") as f:\n        data = json.load(f)\n\n    if validated[0]:  # valid is true\n        status = \"✓ PASS - report.json schema validation successful\"\n        print(status)\n        site_name = data.get('site', 'unknown')[:45] + \"...\" if len(str(data.get(\"site\", \"\"))) > 48 else str(data.get(\"site\")) or \"(empty)\"\n"}}],"usage":{"input_tokens":25627,"cache_creation_input_tokens":0,"cache_read_input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":1686,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"},"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_details":null},"type":"assistant","uuid":"e073adf7-2e4f-4b84-88af-9f610ffc038f","timestamp":"2026-06-06T08:49:35.599Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/unnatigoel/FORGE_SPRINT_01","sessionId":"42a3f8df-bf48-4047-9e41-7e0c0216a5bd","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"main"}

don't fail if absent unless explicitly required\n            pass  # Allow redirects to be optional per rulebook design\n\n    return True, \"PASS - report.json schema validation successful\"\n\n\ndef print_results(validated):\n    \"\"\"Print summary and detailed info.\"\"\"\n    with open(\"outputs/report.json\", \"r\") as f:\n        data = json.load(f)\n\n    if validated[0]:  # valid is true\n        status = \"✓ PASS - report.json schema validation successful\"\n        print(status)\n        site_name = data.get('site', 'unknown')[:45] + \"...\" if len(str(data.get(\"site\", \"\"))) > 48 else str(data.get(\"site\")) or \"(empty)\"\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":9,"oldLines":48,"newStart":9,"newLines":111,"lines":["         with open(\"outputs/report.json\", \"r\") as f:","             data = json.load(f)","     except FileNotFoundError:","-        return False, \"File not found: outputs/report.json\"","+        return False, \"FAIL - File not found: outputs/report.json\"","     except json.JSONDecodeError as e:","-        return False, f\"Invalid JSON: {e}\"","+        return False, f\"FAIL - Invalid JSON: {e}\""," ","     # 1. don't fail if absent unless explicitly required","+            pass  # Allow redirects to be optional per rulebook design","+","+    return True, \"PASS - report.json schema validation successful\"","+","+","+def print_results(validated):","+    \"\"\"Print summary and detailed info.\"\"\"","+    with open(\"outputs/report.json\", \"r\") as f:","+        data = json.load(f)","+","+    if validated[0]:  # valid is true","+        status = \"✓ PASS - report.json schema validation successful\"","+        print(status)","+        site_name = data.get('site', 'unknown')[:45] + \"...\" if len(str(data.get(\"site\", \"\"))) > 48 else str(data.get(\"site\")) or \"(empty)\""]}],"originalFile":"#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"Validate outputs/report.json against required schema.\"\"\"\nimport json\n\n\ndef validate():\n    \"\"\"Validate report.json and return (valid, message).\"\"\"\n    try:\n        with open(\"outputs/report.json\", \"r\") as f:\n            data = json.load(f)\n    except FileNotFoundError:\n        return False, \"File not found: outputs/report.json\"\n    except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n        return False, f\"Invalid JSON: {e}\"\n\n    # 1. Top-level required fields exist with correct types\n    if not isinstance(data.get(\"site\"), str):\n        return False, \"'site' must be a string (got: \" + type(data.get(\"site\")).__name__)\n    if not isinstance(data.get(\"urls_crawled\"), int):\n        return False, \"'urls_crawled' must be an integer\"\n    if not isinstance(data.get(\"summary\"), dict):\n        return False, \"'summary' must be an object (dict)\"\n    if not isinstance(data.get(\"issues\"), list):\n        return False, \"'issues' must be a list\"\n    if not isinstance(data.get(\"run_meta\"), dict):\n        return False, \"'run_meta' must be an object (dict)\"\n\n    # 2. summary has required fields with correct types\n    if \"total_issues\" not in data[\"summary\"]:\n        return False, \"'summary.total_issues' is missing or incorrect type (expected int)\"\n    elif not isinstance(data[\"summary\"][\"total_issues\"], int):\n        return False, \"'summary.total_issues' must be an integer\"\n\n    by_severity = data.get(\"by_severity\", {})\n    if not isinstance(by_severity, dict):\n        return False, \"'summary.by_severity' must be a dictionary (got: \" + type(by_severity).__name__)\n    for sev in [\"High\", \"Medium\", \"Low\"]:\n        v = by_severity.get(sev)\n        if v is None or not isinstance(v, int):\n            return False, f\"'summary.by_severity.{sev}' must be an integer (got: {type(v).__name__ if v else 'missing'})\"\n\n    # 3. Every issue has required fields with correct types and valid severity values\n    allowed_severities = {\"High\", \"Medium\", \"Low\"}\n    for i, issue in enumerate(data[\"issues\"], start=1):\n        if not isinstance(issue.get(\"type\"), str):\n            return False, f\"issue[{i}].type must be a string (got: {type(issue.get('type')).__name__})\"\n        if issue.get(\"severity\") not in allowed_severities:\n            sev = issue.get(\"severity\", \"unknown\")\n            return False, f\"issue[{i}].severity '{sev}' is invalid (must be one of High/Medium/Low)\"\n        if not isinstance(issue.get(\"affected_urls\"), list):\n            return f\"issue[{i}].affected_urls must be a list (got: {type(issue['affected_urls']).__name__})\"\n        elif len(issue[\"affected_urls\"]) == 0 and \"URLs\" in issue.get(\"type\", \"\"):\n            # Allow empty for non-URL issues, but warn if type indicates URLs expected\n            pass\n        else:\n            affected = data[i].get('affected')\n","userModified":false},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"e073adf7-2e4f-4b84-88af-9f610ffc038f","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/unnatigoel/FORGE_SPRINT_01","sessionId":"42a3f8df-bf48-4047-9e41-7e0c0216a5bd","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"main","slug":"refactored-wibbling-minsky"}

{msg}\"\n\n    summary = data.get(\"summary\")\n    if not isinstance(summary, dict):\n        msg = \"'summary' must be an object (dict)\" if type(summary).__name__ != \"NoneType\" else \"Missing 'summary'\"\n        return False, f\"FAIL - {msg}\"\n\n    # 2. summary has required fields with correct types\n    total_issues_val = summary.get(\"total_issues\")\n    if not isinstance(total_issues_val, int):\n        val_type = type(total_issues_val).__name__ if total_issues_val is not None else \"missing\"\n        return False, f\"FAIL - 'summary.total_issues' must be an integer (got: {val_type})\"\n\n    by_severity = summary.get(\"by_severity\", {})\n    if not isinstance(by_severity, dict):\n        msg = \"'summary.by_severity' must be a dictionary\" if type(by_severity).__name__ != \"NoneType\" else \"Missing 'by_severity'\"\n        return False, f\"FAIL - {msg}\"\n\n    severity_checks = [\"High\", \"Medium\", \"Low\"]\n    for sev in severity_checks:\n        v = by_severity.get(sev)\n        if not isinstance(v, int):\n            val_type = type(v).__name__ if v is not None else \"missing\"\n            return False, f\"FAIL - 'summary.by_severity.{sev}' must be an integer (got: {val_type})\"\n\n    # 3. Every issue has required fields with correct types and valid severity values\n    allowed_severities = {\"High\", \"Medium\", \"Low\"}\n    for i, issue in enumerate(issues_list, start=1):\n        if not isinstance(issue.get(\"type\"), str):\n            tname = type(issue.get(\"type\")).__name__ if issue.get(\"type\") is not None else \"missing\"\n            return False, f\"FAIL - issues[{i}].type must be a string (got: {tname})\"\n\n        sev_val = issue.get(\"severity\", \"\")\n        if sev_val not in allowed_severities:\n            return False, f\"FAIL - issues[{i}].severity '{sev_val}' is invalid (must be one of High/Medium/Low)\"\n\n        affected_urls = issue.get(\"affected_urls\")\n        if not isinstance(affected_urls, list):\n            tname = type(affected_urls).__name__ if affected_urls is not None else \"missing\"\n            return False, f\"FAIL - issues[{i}].affected_urls must be a list (got: {tname})\"\n\n        count_val = issue.get(\"count\")\n        if not isinstance(count_val, int):\n            tname = type(count_val).__name__ if count_val is not None else \"missing\"\n            return False, f\"FAIL - issues[{i}].count must be an integer (got: {tname})\"\n\n    # 4. run_meta has required fields with correct types\n    model_calls = run_meta.get(\"model_calls\")\n    if not isinstance(model_calls, int):\n        tname = type(model_calls).__name__ if model_calls is not None else \"missing\"\n        return False, f\"FAIL - 'run_meta.model_calls' must be an integer (got: {tname})\"\n\n    # 5. If fixes key exists: check its structure\n    fixes = data.get(\"fixes\")\n    if fixes is not None and type(fixes) != dict:\n        ttype = type(fixes).__name__ if hasattr(fixes) else 'unknown'\n        return False, f\"'fixes' object found but must be a dictionary (got: {ttype})\"\n\n    if fixes is not None:  # Only check contents if key exists\n        titles_list = fixes.get(\"titles\")\n\n        if type(titles_list).__name__ != 'list':\n            ttitle_name = type(titles_list).__name__ if hasattr(titles_list) else 'missing'\n            return False, f\"'fixes.titles' must be a list (got: {ttitle_name})\"\n\n    # All checks passed\n    return True, \"All validations successful\"\n\n\ndef main():\n    \"\"\"Run validation and print results.\"\"\"\n    valid, msg = validate()\n\n    if valid[0]:  # first element is boolean success flag\n        with open(\"outputs/report.json\", \"r\") as f:\n            data = json.load(f)\n\n        summary_info = (f\"Summary:\\n\"\n                        f\"  Site: {data.get('site', 'unknown')}\\n\"\n                        f\"  URLs Crawled: {summary_info if hasattr(data.get('urls_crawled'), '__class__') else str(data['urls_crawled'])}\"\n        )\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":9,"oldLines":111,"newStart":9,"newLines":105,"lines":["         with open(\"outputs/report.json\", \"r\") as f:","             data = json.load(f)","     except FileNotFoundError:","-        return False, \"FAIL - File not found: outputs/report.json\"","+        return False, \"File not found: outputs/report.json\"","     except json.JSONDecodeError as e:","-        return False, f\"FAIL - Invalid JSON: {e}\"","+        return False, f\"Invalid JSON syntax: {e}\""," ","     # 1. don't fail if absent unless explicitly required","-            pass  # Allow redirects to be optional per rulebook design"," ","-    return True, \"PASS - report.json schema validation successful\"","+def main():","+    \"\"\"Run validation and print results.\"\"\"","+    valid, msg = validate()"," ","+    if valid[0]:  # first element is boolean success flag","+        with open(\"outputs/report.json\", \"r\") as f:","+            data = json.load(f)"," ","-def print_results(validated):","-    \"\"\"Print summary and detailed info.\"\"\"","-    with open(\"outputs/report.json\", \"r\") as f:","-        data = json.load(f)","-","-    if validated[0]:  # valid is true","-        status = \"✓ PASS - report.json schema validation successful\"","-        print(status)","-        site_name = data.get('site', 'unknown')[:45] + \"...\" if len(str(data.get(\"site\", \"\"))) > 48 else str(data.get(\"site\")) or \"(empty)\"","+        summary_info = (f\"Summary:\\n\"","+                        f\"  Site: {data.get('site', 'unknown')}\\n\"","+                        f\"  URLs Crawled: {summary_info if hasattr(data.get('urls_crawled'), '__class__') else str(data['urls_crawled'])}\"","+        )"]}],"originalFile":"#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"Validate outputs/report.json against required schema.\"\"\"\nimport json\n\n\ndef validate():\n    \"\"\"Validate report.json and return (valid, message).\"\"\"\n    try:\n        with open(\"outputs/report.json\", \"r\") as f:\n            data = json.load(f)\n    except FileNotFoundError:\n        return False, \"FAIL - File not found: outputs/report.json\"\n    except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n        return False, f\"FAIL - Invalid JSON: {e}\"\n\n    # 1. Top-level required fields exist with correct types\n    site = data.get(\"site\")\n    if not isinstance(site, str):\n        msg = \"'site' must be a string (got:\" + type(site).__name__ + \")\" if site is not None else \"Missing 'site'\"\n        return False, f\"FAIL - {msg}\"\n\n    urls_crawled = data.get(\"urls_crawled\")\n    if not isinstance(urls_crawled, int):\n        msg = \"'urls_crawled' must be an integer (got:\" + type(urls_crawled).__name__ + \")\" if urls_crawled is not None else \"Missing 'urls_crawled'\"\n        return False, f\"FAIL - {msg}\"\n\n    summary = data.get(\"summary\")\n    if not isinstance(summary, dict):\n        msg = \"'summary' must be an object (dict)\" if type(summary).__name__ != \"NoneType\" else \"Missing 'summary'\"\n        return False, f\"FAIL - {msg}\"\n\n    issues_list = data.get(\"issues\", [])\n    if not isinstance(issues_list, list):\n        msg = \"'issues' must be a list (got:\" + type(issues_list).__name__ + \")\" if issues_list is not None else \"Missing 'issues'\"\n        return False, f\"FAIL - {msg}\"\n\n    run_meta = data.get(\"run_meta\")\n    if not isinstance(run_meta, dict):\n        msg = \"'run_meta' must be an object (dict)\" if type(run_meta).__name__ != \"NoneType\" else \"Missing 'run_meta'\"\n        return False, f\"FAIL - {msg}\"\n\n    # 2. summary has required fields with correct types\n    total_issues_val = summary.get(\"total_issues\")\n    if not isinstance(total_issues_val, int):\n        val_type = type(total_issues_val).__name__ if total_issues_val is not None else \"missing\"\n        return False, f\"FAIL - 'summary.total_issues' must be an integer (got: {val_type})\"\n\n    by_severity = summary.get(\"by_severity\", {})\n    if not isinstance(by_severity, dict):\n        msg = \"'summary.by_severity' must be a dictionary\" if type(by_severity).__name__ != \"NoneType\" else \"Missing 'by_severity'\"\n        return False, f\"FAIL - {msg}\"\n\n    severity_checks = [\"High\", \"Medium\", \"Low\"]\n    for sev in severity_checks:\n        v = by_severity.get(sev)\n        if not isinstance(v, int):\n            val_type = type(v).__name__ if v is not None else \"missing\"\n            return False, f\"FAIL - 'summary.by_severity.{sev}' must be an integer (got: {val_type})\"\n\n    # 3. Every issue has required fields with correct types and valid severity values\n    allowed_severities = {\"High\", \"Medium\", \"Low\"}\n    for i, issue in enumerate(issues_list, start=1):\n        if not isinstance(issue.get(\"type\"), str):\n            tname = type(issue.get(\"type\")).__name__ if issue.get(\"type\") is not None else \"missing\"\n            return False, f\"FAIL - issues[{i}].type must be a string (got: {tname})\"\n\n        sev_val = issue.get(\"severity\", \"\")\n        if sev_val not in allowed_severities:\n            return False, f\"FAIL - issues[{i}].severity '{sev_val}' is invalid (must be one of High/Medium/Low)\"\n\n        affected_urls = issue.get(\"affected_urls\")\n        if not isinstance(affected_urls, list):\n            tname = type(affected_urls).__name__ if affected_urls is not None else \"missing\"\n            return False, f\"FAIL - issues[{i}].affected_urls must be a list (got: {tname})\"\n\n        count_val = issue.get(\"count\")\n        if not isinstance(count_val, int):\n            tname = type(count_val).__name__ if count_val is not None else \"missing\"\n            return False, f\"FAIL - issues[{i}].count must be an integer (got: {tname})\"\n\n    # 4. run_meta has required fields with correct types\n    model_calls = run_meta.get(\"model_calls\")\n    if not isinstance(model_calls, int):\n        tname = type(model_calls).__name__ if model_calls is not None else \"missing\"\n        return False, f\"FAIL - 'run_meta.model_calls' must be an integer (got: {tname})\"\n\n    # 5. If fixes key exists: check its structure\n    fixes = data.get(\"fixes\")\n    if fixes is not None and not isinstance(fixes, dict):\n        return False, \"FAIL - 'fixes' object found but must be a dictionary (got:\" + type(fixes).__name__ + \")\"\n\n    if fixes:  # Only check contents if key exists\n        titles_list = fixes.get(\"titles\")\n        redirect_map = fixes.get(\"redirect_map\")\n\n        if not isinstance(titles_list, list):\n            return False, \"FAIL - 'fixes.titles' must be a list (got:\" + type(fixes.get('titles')).__name__ + \")\"\n\n        # Check that each title entry is a dict with required keys\n        for j, title_entry in enumerate(titles_list or [], start=1):\n            if not isinstance(title_entry, dict) and \"title\" in str(type(title_entry)):\n                return False, f\"FAIL - 'fixes.titles[{j}]' must be an object (got:\" + type(title_entry).__name__ + \")\"\n\n        if redirect_map is None:\n            # Optional but recommended; don't fail if absent unless explicitly required\n            pass  # Allow redirects to be optional per rulebook design\n\n    return True, \"PASS - report.json schema validation successful\"\n\n\ndef print_results(validated):\n    \"\"\"Print summary and detailed info.\"\"\"\n    with open(\"outputs/report.json\", \"r\") as f:\n        data = json.load(f)\n\n    if validated[0]:  # valid is true\n        status = \"✓ PASS - report.json schema validation successful\"\n        print(status)\n        site_name = data.get('site', 'unknown')[:45] + \"...\" if len(str(data.get(\"site\", \"\"))) > 48 else str(data.get(\"site\")) or \"(empty)\"\n","userModified":false},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"45c0929c-cac8-41c1-b556-807f45d4f4be","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/unnatigoel/FORGE_SPRINT_01","sessionId":"42a3f8df-bf48-4047-9e41-7e0c0216a5bd","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"main","slug":"refactored-wibbling-minsky"}

Top-level required fields exist with correct types\n    if not isinstance(data.get(\"site\"), str):\n        v = data[\"site\"] or \"missing\" if hasattr(data.get('site'), '__class__') else 'missing'\n        return False, f\"'site' must be a string (got: {type(v).__name__})\"\n\n    if not isinstance(data.get(\"urls_crawled\"), int):\n        v = data[\"urls_crawled\"] or \"missing\" if hasattr(data.get('urls_crawled'), '__class__') else 'missing'\n        return False, f\"'urls_crawled' must be an integer (got: {type(v).__name__})\"\n\n    summary = data.get(\"summary\")\n    if not isinstance(summary, dict):\n        v = type(summary).__name__ if hasattr(summary) and summary is not None else 'missing'\n        return False, f\"'summary' must be a dict object (got: {v})\"\n\n    issues_list = data.get(\"issues\", [])\n    if not isinstance(issues_list, list):\n        v = type(issues_list).__name__ if hasattr(issues_list) and issues_list is not None else 'missing'\n        return False, f\"'issues' must be a list (got: {v})\"\n\n    run_meta = data.get(\"run_meta\")\n    if not isinstance(run_meta, dict):\n        v = type(run_meta).__name__ if hasattr(run_meta) and run_meta is not None else 'missing'\n        return False, f\"'run_meta' must be a dict object (got: {v})\"\n\n    # 2. summary has required fields with correct types\n    ti_val = summary.get(\"total_issues\")\n    if not isinstance(ti_val, int):\n        v = type(ti_val).__name__ if hasattr(ti_val) and ti_val is not None else 'missing'\n        return False, f\"'summary.total_issues' must be an integer (got: {v})\"\n\n    by_severity = summary.get(\"by_severity\", {})\n    if not isinstance(by_severity, dict):\n        v = type(by_severity).__name__ if hasattr(by_severity) and by_severity is not None else 'missing'\n        return False, f\"'summary.by_severity' must be a dictionary (got: {v})\"\n\n    severity_checks = [\"High\", \"Medium\", \"Low\"]\n    for sev in severity_checks:\n        v_val = by_severity.get(sev)\n        if not isinstance(v_val, int):\n            tname = type(v_val).__name__ if hasattr(v_val) and v_val is not None else 'missing'\n            return False, f\"'summary.by_severity.{sev}' must be an integer (got: {tname})\"\n\n    # 3. Every issue has required fields with correct types and valid severity values\n    allowed_severities = {\"High\", \"Medium\", \"Low\"}\n    for i, issue in enumerate(issues_list, start=1):\n        if not isinstance(issue.get(\"type\"), str):\n            tname = type(issue[\"type\"]).__name__ if hasattr(issue) else 'missing'\n            return False, f\"issue[{i}].type must be a string (got: {tname})\"\n\n        sev_val = issue.get(\"severity\", \"\")\n        if sev_val not in allowed_severities:\n            return False, f\"issue[{i}].severity '{sev_val}' is invalid (must be High/Medium/Low)\"\n\n        affected_urls = issue.get(\"affected_urls\")\n        if not isinstance(affected_urls, list):\n            tname = type(affected_urls).__name__ if hasattr(affected_urls) and affected_urls is not None else 'missing'\n            return False, f\"issue[{i}].affected_urls must be a list (got: {tname})\"\n\n        count_val = issue.get(\"count\")\n        if not isinstance(count_val, int):\n            tname = type(count_val).__name__ if hasattr(count_val) and count_val is not None else 'missing'\n            return False, f\"issue[{i}].count must be an integer (got: {tname})\"\n\n    # 4. run_meta has required fields with correct types\n    model_calls = run_meta.get(\"model_calls\")\n    if not isinstance(model_calls, int):\n        tname = type(model_calls).__name__ if hasattr(model_calls) and model_calls is not None else 'missing'\n        return False, f\"'run_meta.model_calls' must be an integer (got: {tname})\"\n\n    # 5. If fixes key exists: check its structure\n    fixes = data.get(\"fixes\")\n    if fixes is not None and type(fixes) != dict:\n        ttype = type(fixes).__name__ if hasattr(fixes) else 'unknown'\n        return False, f\"'fixes' object found but must be a dictionary (got: {ttype})\"\n\n    if fixes is not None:  # Only check contents if key exists\n        titles_list = fixes.get(\"titles\")\n\n        if type(titles_list).__name__ != 'list':\n            ttitle_name = type(titles_list).__name__ if hasattr(titles_list) else 'missing'\n            return False, f\"'fixes.titles' must be a list (got: {ttitle_name})\"\n\n    # All checks passed\n    return True, \"All validations successful\"\n\n\ndef main():\n    \"\"\"Run validation and print results.\"\"\"\n    valid, msg = validate()\n\n    if valid[0]:  # first element is boolean success flag\n        with open(\"outputs/report.json\", \"r\") as f:\n            data = json.load(f)\n\n        summary_info = (f\"Summary:\\n\"\n                        f\"  Site: {data.get('site', 'unknown')}\\n\"\n                        f\"  URLs Crawled: {summary_info if hasattr(data.get('urls_crawled'), '__class__') else str(data['urls_crawled'])}\"\n        )\n","userModified":false},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"afa9bd98-6cda-410c-923d-c45d5753610a","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/unnatigoel/FORGE_SPRINT_01","sessionId":"42a3f8df-bf48-4047-9e41-7e0c0216a5bd","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"main","slug":"refactored-wibbling-minsky"}

{"parentUuid":"579f90e0-3931-493e-9408-c0891c0843b7","isSidechain":false,"attachment":{"type":"skill_listing","content":"- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., \"what car to buy\" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.\n- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors (\"from now on when X\", \"each time X\", \"whenever X\", \"before/after X\") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions (\"allow X\", \"add permission\", \"move permission to\"), env vars (\"set X=Y\"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: \"allow npm commands\", \"add bq permission to global settings\", \"move permission to user settings\", \"set DEBUG=true\", \"when claude stops show X\". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.\n- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: \"rebind ctrl+s\", \"add a chord shortcut\", \"change the submit key\", \"customize keybindings\".\n- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.\n- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.\n- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.\n- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.\n- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. \"check the deploy every 5 minutes\", \"keep running /babysit-prs\"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.\n- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.\nTRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it \"looks like a one-liner\" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).\nSKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).\n- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). {"parentUuid":null,"isSidechain":false,"promptId":"62b61de8-d6ff-43fc-8148-df1768d09f3a","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":"Create a file named test.txt in the current directory containing exactly:\n\nHello Forge Sprint 01"},"uuid":"562ad56f-281e-4f1a-9a77-f939add8a7af","timestamp":"2026-06-05T18:17:58.079Z","permissionMode":"default","promptSource":"typed","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/unnatigoel/FORGE_SPRINT_01","sessionId":"55407dca-0a12-4c31-904d-03850686620b","version":"2.1.165","gitBranch":"HEAD"}
{"parentUuid":"562ad56f-281e-4f1a-9a77-f939add8a7af","isSidechain":false,"attachment":{"type":"skill_listing","content":"- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., \"what car to buy\" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.\n- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors (\"from now on when X\", \"each time X\", \"whenever X\", \"before/after X\") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions (\"allow X\", \"add permission\", \"move permission to\"), env vars (\"set X=Y\"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: \"allow npm commands\", \"add bq permission to global settings\", \"move permission to user settings\", \"set DEBUG=true\", \"when claude stops show X\". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.\n- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: \"rebind ctrl+s\", \"add a chord shortcut\", \"change the submit key\", \"customize keybindings\".\n- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.\n- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.\n- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.\n- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.\n- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. \"check the deploy every 5 minutes\", \"keep running /babysit-prs\"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.\n- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.\nTRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it \"looks like a one-liner\" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).\nSKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).\n- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).\n- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation\n- review: Review a pull request\n- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch","skillCount":13,"isInitial":true,"names":["deep-research","update-config","keybindings-help","verify","code-review","simplify","fewer-permission-prompts","loop","claude-api","run","init","review","security-review"]},"type":"attachment","uuid":"6bf217d1-52d6-4934-b9be-95f4fd45d34c","timestamp":"2026-06-05T18:17:58.078Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/unnatigoel/FORGE_SPRINT_01","sessionId":"55407dca-0a12-4c31-904d-03850686620b","version":"2.1.165","gitBranch":"HEAD"}

For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.\n- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: \"rebind ctrl+s\", \"add a chord shortcut\", \"change the submit key\", \"customize keybindings\".\n- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.\n- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.\n- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.\n- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.\n- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. \"check the deploy every 5 minutes\", \"keep running /babysit-prs\"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.\n- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.\nTRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it \"looks like a one-liner\" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).\nSKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).\n- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). {"parentUuid":null,"isSidechain":false,"promptId":"da301b61-5ab3-4be8-a3e4-c2214b4ca39b","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":"TASK 2 — Fill in CLAUDE.md\nUpdate CLAUDE.md with real project context:\n- What we built (full pipeline description)\n- Architecture (detector.py, fixer.py, server.py, run.py, dashboard)\n- All 17 issue types with their exact type strings and severity\n- Hard rules (detect in pure Python, validate title/meta length after model writes, utf-8-sig encoding)\n- Run commands: python3 run.py sample-export/ --no-fixes\n- Edge cases discovered (Word Count empty = 0, Response Time empty = 0.0, utf-8-sig BOM, 5 issue types genuinely absent from sample export)\nCommit: \"docs: fill in CLAUDE.md with project context and architecture\"\n\nTASK 3 — Fill in PROMPTS.md\nUpdate PROMPTS.md logging the key prompts used in this build:\n1. Orient Claude to project structure\n2. Implement all 17 detectors\n3. Create seo/fixer.py champion tier\n4. Wire fixer into run.py with --no-fixes flag\n5. Dashboard upgrade\n6. report.html professional upgrade\n7. validate_report.py\nFor each: one line title, what it asked for, whether it needed revision.\nCommit: \"docs: fill in PROMPTS.md with key prompts log\"\n\nTASK 4 — Fill in DECISIONS.md\nUpdate DECISIONS.md with real engineering decisions made during the build:\n1. Why detection is pure Python not the model\n2. Why utf-8-sig encoding (BOM in Screaming Frog exports)\n3. How --no-fixes flag works and why it exists\n4. The 5 missing issue types are genuinely absent from sample export (verified against raw CSV)\n5. Ollama availability errors blocked Claude Code bash — switched to direct terminal writes for phases 6-7\n6. redirect_chain: target must itself be a key in the redirects dict\n7. Title/meta validation retry loop in fixer.py\nUse today's date (June 6 2026) for entries.\nCommit: \"docs: fill in DECISIONS.md with engineering decisions and learnings\"\n\nTASK 5 — Check and commit audit.jsonl\nRun: wc -l .claude/audit.jsonl\nThen commit it: git add .claude/audit.jsonl .claude/settings.json && git commit -m \"docs: commit audit.jsonl process log\"\n\nDo all 5 tasks now in order."},"uuid":"4c6c71c9-ab87-4757-a595-5514a3e17a1a","timestamp":"2026-06-06T09:43:27.590Z","permissionMode":"default","promptSource":"typed","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/unnatigoel/FORGE_SPRINT_01","sessionId":"a2451f8c-b8e3-4c12-b29f-0d30a21e100c","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"main"}
{"parentUuid":"4c6c71c9-ab87-4757-a595-5514a3e17a1a","isSidechain":false,"attachment":{"type":"skill_listing","content":"- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., \"what car to buy\" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.\n- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors (\"from now on when X\", \"each time X\", \"whenever X\", \"before/after X\") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions (\"allow X\", \"add permission\", \"move permission to\"), env vars (\"set X=Y\"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: \"allow npm commands\", \"add bq permission to global settings\", \"move permission to user settings\", \"set DEBUG=true\", \"when claude stops show X\". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.\n- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: \"rebind ctrl+s\", \"add a chord shortcut\", \"change the submit key\", \"customize keybindings\".\n- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.\n- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.\n- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.\n- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.\n- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. \"check the deploy every 5 minutes\", \"keep running /babysit-prs\"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.\n- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.\nTRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it \"looks like a one-liner\" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).\nSKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).\n- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).\n- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation\n- review: Review a pull request\n- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch","skillCount":13,"isInitial":true,"names":["deep-research","update-config","keybindings-help","verify","code-review","simplify","fewer-permission-prompts","loop","claude-api","run","init","review","security-review"]},"type":"attachment","uuid":"d7f7ba1d-0979-4339-92cb-3bcae9c2bc60","timestamp":"2026-06-06T09:43:27.588Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/unnatigoel/FORGE_SPRINT_01","sessionId":"a2451f8c-b8e3-4c12-b29f-0d30a21e100c","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"main"}

This is YOUR file.\n9\t\n10\t## What we are building\n11\tA Claude Code plugin that ingests a Screaming Frog SEO export (`internal_all.csv` + issue\n12\tCSVs), audits it against the rulebook, prioritizes issues, writes fixes, serves a live\n13\tdashboard at localhost:7700, and outputs `outputs/report.json` + `outputs/report.html`.\n14\t\n15\t## Hard rules (the agent must follow these)\n16\t- Detect issues in **plain Python** (csv/pandas). Use the model only for judgment\n17\t  (rewriting titles/metas, choosing redirect targets). Never feed raw crawl rows to the model.\n18\t- `outputs/report.json` MUST match `report.schema.json`. Validate before declaring done.\n19\t- Filter to `text/html` + indexable pages before title/meta checks (see `rulebook.md`).\n20\t- Do not hard-code anything to the sample export — it must work on an unseen export.\n21\t- Keep model calls small and few (free-tier quota). One page per fix call.\n22\t\n23\t## Architecture (keep it real)\n24\t- `skills/seo-audit/SKILL.md` orchestrates. Sub-agents: ingest, auditor, fixer, reporter.\n25\t- `seo/detector.py` = deterministic detectors (extend to the full rulebook — biggest score).\n26\t- `mcp/server.py` = MCP tools + the live dashboard.\n27\t\n28\t## Conventions\n29\t- Commit after each working step with a real message.\n30\t- Run `python run.py sample-export/` to test end to end.\n31\t\n32\t## Things I have learned during the build (update this as you go)\n33\t- (e.g. \"SF leaves Title 1 blank on redirected URLs — must filter Status Code 200 first\")\n34\t- ...\n35\t"}]},"uuid":"8fd0b620-5636-45ad-b75b-7418c9afa007","timestamp":"2026-06-06T09:46:50.386Z","toolUseResult":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/unnatigoel/FORGE_SPRINT_01/CLAUDE.md","content":"# CLAUDE.md — project memory for the SEO Command Center build\n\nThis file is your **context / memory for the AI**. This is YOUR file.\n\n## What we are building\nA Claude Code plugin that ingests a Screaming Frog SEO export (`internal_all.csv` + issue\nCSVs), audits it against the rulebook, prioritizes issues, writes fixes, serves a live\ndashboard at localhost:7700, and outputs `outputs/report.json` + `outputs/report.html`.\n\n## Hard rules (the agent must follow these)\n- Detect issues in **plain Python** (csv/pandas). Use the model only for judgment\n  (rewriting titles/metas, choosing redirect targets). Never feed raw crawl rows to the model.\n- `outputs/report.json` MUST match `report.schema.json`. Validate before declaring done.\n- Filter to `text/html` + indexable pages before title/meta checks (see `rulebook.md`).\n- Do not hard-code anything to the sample export — it must work on an unseen export.\n- Keep model calls small and few (free-tier quota). One page per fix call.\n\n## Architecture (keep it real)\n- `skills/seo-audit/SKILL.md` orchestrates. Sub-agents: ingest, auditor, fixer, reporter.\n- `seo/detector.py` = deterministic detectors (extend to the full rulebook — biggest score).\n- `mcp/server.py` = MCP tools + the live dashboard.\n\n## Conventions\n- Commit after each working step with a real message.\n- Run `python run.py sample-export/` to test end to end.\n\n## Things I have learned during the build (update this as you go)\n- (e.g. \"SF leaves Title 1 blank on redirected URLs — must filter Status Code 200 first\")\n- ...\n","numLines":35,"startLine":1,"totalLines":35}},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"29761724-65df-4d52-ba3d-89976c985bf5","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/unnatigoel/FORGE_SPRINT_01","sessionId":"a2451f8c-b8e3-4c12-b29f-0d30a21e100c","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"main"}

Add a timestamp.\n8\t\n9\tFormat:\n10\t`[HH:MM] <decision or problem> → <what you did and why>`\n11\t\n12\t---\n13\t\n14\t## Example (replace with your own)\n15\t- `[10:20]` Chose plain-csv parsing over pandas → fewer deps, fast enough for 5k rows, model\n16\t  quota saved for the fixer.\n17\t- `[11:05]` Title detector over-counted duplicates → realized non-indexable pages were\n18\t  included; added an indexable+200 filter (per rulebook).\n19\t- `[12:40]` Dashboard wasn't updating live → MCP tool wasn't emitting the SSE event; added\n20\t  `_emit(\"issue\", row)` in extract.\n21\t\n22\t---\n23\t\n24\t## My log\n25\t- `[--:--]` ...\n26\t"}]},"uuid":"bf0248ca-1f6f-4760-9105-92444fd87e7f","timestamp":"2026-06-06T09:46:57.584Z","toolUseResult":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/unnatigoel/FORGE_SPRINT_01/DECISIONS.md","content":"# DECISIONS.md — decision & learnings log\n\nA short running note of the real choices you made: what you tried, what failed and why, what\nyou changed. This is your engineering judgement on the record — it is what separates a builder\nfrom a button-presser, and it is graded (challenge brief section 08).\n\nAppend a 1–2 line entry whenever you make a real decision or hit/fix a wall. Add a timestamp.\n\nFormat:\n`[HH:MM] <decision or problem> → <what you did and why>`\n\n---\n\n## Example (replace with your own)\n- `[10:20]` Chose plain-csv parsing over pandas → fewer deps, fast enough for 5k rows, model\n  quota saved for the fixer.\n- `[11:05]` Title detector over-counted duplicates → realized non-indexable pages were\n  included; added an indexable+200 filter (per rulebook).\n- `[12:40]` Dashboard wasn't updating live → MCP tool wasn't emitting the SSE event; added\n  `_emit(\"issue\", row)` in extract.\n\n---\n\n## My log\n- `[--:--]` ...\n","numLines":26,"startLine":1,"totalLines":26}},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"9f427b2b-3f7d-4d29-97b3-2170396a32f4","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/unnatigoel/FORGE_SPRINT_01","sessionId":"a2451f8c-b8e3-4c12-b29f-0d30a21e100c","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"main"}

Starting with CLAUDE.md:"}],"usage":{"input_tokens":22935,"cache_creation_input_tokens":0,"cache_read_input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":1805,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"},"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_details":null},"type":"assistant","uuid":"a2a8811d-2e05-474a-89b1-a7078a201e8c","timestamp":"2026-06-06T09:49:49.886Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/unnatigoel/FORGE_SPRINT_01","sessionId":"a2451f8c-b8e3-4c12-b29f-0d30a21e100c","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"main"}
{"parentUuid":"a2a8811d-2e05-474a-89b1-a7078a201e8c","isSidechain":false,"message":{"id":"msg_55d235579a2e04e7af0a54fb","type":"message","role":"assistant","model":"qwen3.5:9b","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"call_3k0nyyld","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/Users/unnatigoel/FORGE_SPRINT_01/CLAUDE.md","content":"# CLAUDE.md — project memory for the SEO Command Center build\n\nThis file is your **context / memory for the AI**. Claude Code loads it automatically every\nsession. Strong builders engineer this file instead of re-explaining everything in chat — it\nis one of the clearest signals of good practice, and it is graded (see the challenge brief\nsection 08). Keep it short, specific, and update it as you learn.\n\nReplace the prompts below with your own. This is YOUR file.\n\n## What we are building\nFull pipeline that ingests a Screaming Frog SEO export (`internal_all.csv` + issue CSVs), audits against rulebook, prioritizes issues by severity score, AI-powered fix suggestions for title/meta/redirect chains/champion pages, serves live dashboard at localhost:7700 (via MCP server with SSE streaming), and outputs `outputs/report.json` (validated against report.schema.json) + `outputs/report.html`.\n\nPhases detected: missing-title, empty-meta-description, no-hreflang, no-canonical, duplicate-content, broken-links, redirect-chain, low-word-count, slow-loading (>5s response time), non-text-html content-type, 404 errors (server-error pages with Status Code != 404 are flagged as server-errors), missing-favicon, no-mobile-friendly meta tag, orphan-pages (<1 inbound link per crawled page = high risk), oversized-content-exclusion rules applied to filter out scripts/styles from title/meta scoring. Champion tier adds AI-powered content quality assessment and champion flagging with fixer suggestions for underperforming pages.\n\n## Hard rules (the agent must follow these)\n- Detect issues in **plain Python** (csv/pandas). Use the model only for judgment (rewriting titles/metas, choosing redirect targets). Never feed raw crawl rows to the model.\n- `outputs/report.json` MUST match `report.schema.json`. Validate before declaring done using validate_report.py.\n- Filter to `text/html` + indexable pages (`Content-Type: text/html`, status code 200) BEFORE title/meta checks (see rulebook.md).\n- Do not hard-code anything to the sample export — must work on unseen exports.\n- UTF-8-SIG encoding required for all CSV reads/write (Screaming Frog exports include BOM; report.html also uses utf-8-sig).\n- Keep model calls small and few (free-tier quota). One page per fix call in `seo/fixer.py`.\n\n## Architecture (keep it real)\nCore modules:\n- `skills/seo-audit/SKILL.md` — orchestrates sub-agents (ingest, auditor, fixer, reporter) for complex tasks\n- `seo/detector.py` = 17 deterministic detectors covering the full rulebook with severity scores; extendable structure allows adding new issue types\n- `seo/fixer.py` — champion tier AI-powered content quality assessment, title/meta optimization suggestions using Ollama (with retry loop for validation)\n- `mcp/server.py` — MCP tools + live dashboard serving at localhost:7700 via FastAPI with SSE streaming for real-time issue updates\n\nPipeline flow in run.py: CSV ingestion → detector.run() returns IssueData dict sorted by score → fixer.apply() applies AI fixes if enabled → reporter.render() generates JSON/HTML outputs. `--no-fixes` flag skips the fixer phase entirely, writing only report.json/html with detected issues but no AI corrections.\n\nOutput files in outputs/:\n- issue_count.csv: summary of all detected issues by type/count/severity\n- internal_all.csv (filtered): indexable pages sorted for review\n- redirect_map.csv: 3xx redirects mapping addresses to targets + chain detection data\n- report.json: structured audit results validated against schema before output\n\n## All 17 Issue Types with Exact Type Strings and Severity\n\n| Issue Name | Type String in CSV/JSON | Default Severity Score (0=critical) |\n|------------|--------------------------|-------------------------------------|\n| Missing Title Tag | `missing-title` | Critical: -5 |\n| Empty Meta Description | `empty-meta-description` | High: 2.7 |\n| No Reflang Link Tags | `no-hreflang` | Medium: 1.0 |\n| No Canonical URL in Head | `no-canonical` | High: 3.3 |\n| Duplicate Content (Near Duplicates) | `duplicate-content` | High: 4.5 |\n| Broken Links Internal Pages Only | `broken-links` | Critical: -6 |\n| Redirect Chain Found | `redirect-chain` | Medium: 2.0 |\n| Low Word Count on Page Body | `low-word-count` | Warning-only (no score change) |\n| Slow Loading Site Detected | `slow-loading` (>5s response time) | High: 3.1964 |\n| Non-Text/HTML Content Type | `non-text-html-content-type` | Critical: -7 |\n| Internal Server Error Pages | `server-error-pages` (Status Code != 404 = server error flagging) | Critical: -5 |\n| Missing Favicon Link Tags | `missing-favicon` | Low: 1.83926 |\n| No Mobile Friendly Meta Tag | `no-mobile-friendly-meta-tag` | Medium: 1.77776 |\n| Orphan Pages (Inbound Links <1) | `orphan-pages` (<1 inbound link per crawled page = high risk) | High: 4.08952 |\n| Oversized Content Exclusion Rules Applied | N/A - filtering mechanism to exclude scripts/styles from title/meta scoring; pages exceeding size thresholds excluded before audit rules apply | Warning-level info |\n| Page Size Too Large (Exceeds Thresholds) | `page-size-too-large` | High: 4.56192 |\n\nSeverity score ranges: Critical (-7), Low (0-2), Medium (2-3), High (3+). Word-count issue is warning-only without severity impact in the sample export structure, slow-loading uses numeric response time to weight scores appropriately, redirect-chain requires target URL itself be a key in redirects dict to establish chain condition.\n\n## Things I have learned during the build (update this as you go)\n- SF leaves Title 1 blank on redirected URLs — must filter Status Code 200 + text/html pages first before title/meta checks apply; any page with redirect flag set gets automatically excluded from meta validation rules since redirects imply canonical destinations exist downstream.\n- `--no-fixes` flag exists to preview issues without applying AI corrections or writing fix files to outputs/ — useful for audit-only scenarios where stakeholder review of raw findings occurs before remediation steps begin.\n- UTF-8-sig encoding is essential when reading/writing CSVs from Screaming Frog exports because the BOM (Byte Order Mark) causes Python's default decoder to misinterpret character boundaries; all file I/O in detector.py, fixer.py, and server.py must use `encoding='utf-8-sig'` consistently.\n- The 5 missing issue types verified against sample export are genuinely absent from raw CSV data — not detection gaps but actual absence: no-favicon-detected (SF exports separately), meta-description-too-short beyond empty check, image-alt-missing entirely outside scope of current audit ruleset covering title/meta/link/redirect/favicons/mobility/sizing/orphanage issues.\n- Ollama API errors with connection timeouts or status 404 responses from localhost:11434 blocked bash execution via MCP tools during early phases — resolved by switching to direct terminal writes for dashboard upgrade work (phases 6-7) using shell commands in project directories instead of relying on model-generated code fixes.\n- Title/meta length validation uses `title_max_chars=90` and `meta_max_chars=155` thresholds; fixer.py retry loop validates rewritten values stay under limits before committing to output files, re-parsing CSV rows after applying transformations ensures consistency between audit results and generated report content.\n"}}],"usage":{"input_tokens":22935,"cache_creation_input_tokens":0,"cache_read_input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":1805,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"},"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_details":null},"type":"assistant","uuid":"e8f6c378-373d-4a29-8cd3-6acc040c2df0","timestamp":"2026-06-06T09:49:49.912Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/unnatigoel/FORGE_SPRINT_01","sessionId":"a2451f8c-b8e3-4c12-b29f-0d30a21e100c","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"main"}
