{"parentUuid":"c69689c0-5198-41e4-a1bd-fdf8fb7f7ac0","isSidechain":false,"type":"system","subtype":"away_summary","content":"Goal: Implement 10 missing SEO detectors in rulebook.md using plain Python. Currently wrote partial code but it's incomplete (got truncated mid-function). Next action: write the complete, correct `detector.py` with all 17 detectors properly implemented by reading from my earlier context and rewriting fully. (disable recaps in /config)","timestamp":"2026-06-06T07:17:22.225Z","uuid":"37e571fa-e938-4750-83eb-df9ed84fd344","isMeta":false,"userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/unnatigoel/FORGE_SPRINT_01","sessionId":"a5703151-609a-458e-9f13-68062fc3f616","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"main","slug":"enchanted-dancing-quokka"}
{"parentUuid":"37e571fa-e938-4750-83eb-df9ed84fd344","isSidechain":false,"promptId":"585b2f00-0c12-4f52-b5bd-c3869d972496","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":"Create a new file seo/fixer.py. This adds champion-tier AI fixes using the local Ollama model.\n\nHere is exactly what it must do:\n\nFUNCTION: run_fixes(issues, rows) -> dict\nReturns: {\"titles\": [...], \"redirect_map\": [...], \"model_calls\": 0}\n\nPART 1 — Ollama API call (standard library only, no requests/httpx):\ndef _call_ollama(prompt):\n    import urllib.request, json\n    payload = json.dumps({\n        \"model\": \"qwen3.5:9b\",\n        \"prompt\": prompt,\n        \"stream\": False,\n        \"options\": {\"num_predict\": 80, \"temperature\": 0.3}\n    }).encode()\n    req = urllib.request.Request(\n        \"http://localhost:11434/api/generate\",\n        data=payload,\n        headers={\"Content-Type\": \"application/json\"}\n    )\n    try:\n        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=30) as r:\n            return json.loads(r.read()).get(\"response\", \"\").strip()\n    except Exception:\n        return \"\"\n\nPART 2 — Build a URL index for fast lookup:\ndef _index(rows):\n    return {r[\"Address\"]: r for r in rows}\n\nPART 3 — Title rewrites:\nFor issue types \"missing_title\" and \"title_too_long\":\n- Get affected_urls from the issue\n- Only process first 15 URLs (to keep quota low)\n- For each URL, look it up in the row index\n- Get old_title = row.get(\"Title 1\", \"\"), h1 = row.get(\"H1-1\", \"\")\n- Call _call_ollama with:\n  \"Write an SEO page title for this webpage. Maximum 60 characters. Return ONLY the title text, nothing else, no quotes, no explanation.\n  URL: {url}\n  H1 heading: {h1}\n  Current title: {old_title}\"\n- If len(new_title) > 60, call _call_ollama again with:\n  \"Shorten this title to under 60 characters. Return ONLY the shortened title, nothing else.\n  Title: {new_title}\"\n- Append {\"url\": url, \"old\": old_title, \"new\": new_title} to titles list\n- Increment model_calls each time _call_ollama is called\n\nPART 4 — Meta description rewrites:\nFor issue types \"missing_meta_description\" and \"meta_description_too_long\":\n- Same pattern, first 15 URLs only\n- Max 155 characters\n- Prompt:\n  \"Write an SEO meta description for this webpage. Maximum 155 characters. Return ONLY the description text, nothing else, no quotes.\n  URL: {url}\n  Title: {title}\n  H1 heading: {h1}\"\n- Retry if len > 155\n\nPART 5 — Redirect map for broken links:\nFor issue type \"broken_link\":\n- Get the list of 4xx URLs from the issue\n- Build list of live pages: all rows where Status Code == 200 and Indexability == Indexable\n- For each broken URL:\n  - Split its path by \"/\" to get path parts\n  - For each live page, count how many path parts match\n  - Pick the live page with the most matching parts\n  - Append {\"from\": broken_url, \"to\": best_match, \"reason\": \"404 → closest live page by path\"}\n\nWrap everything in try/except so if Ollama is slow or down, it skips and continues.\nWrite the complete seo/fixer.py file."},"uuid":"479f9646-3f58-4b21-8aeb-c3ccc39460a6","timestamp":"2026-06-06T07:25:13.636Z","permissionMode":"auto","promptSource":"typed","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/unnatigoel/FORGE_SPRINT_01","sessionId":"a5703151-609a-458e-9f13-68062fc3f616","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"main","slug":"enchanted-dancing-quokka"}
{"parentUuid":"479f9646-3f58-4b21-8aeb-c3ccc39460a6","isSidechain":false,"attachment":{"type":"edited_text_file","filename":"/Users/unnatigoel/FORGE_SPRINT_01/seo/detector.py","snippet":"1\t\"\"\"\n2\tdetector.py — deterministic SEO issue detection from a Screaming Frog internal_all.csv.\n3\tCovers all 17 issue types from rulebook.md.\n4\t\"\"\"\n5\tfrom __future__ import annotations\n6\timport csv\n7\timport os\n8\tfrom collections import defaultdict\n9\t\n10\t\n11\tdef load_rows(export_dir: str) -> list[dict]:\n12\t    path = os.path.join(export_dir, \"internal_all.csv\")\n13\t    with open(path, encoding=\"utf-8-sig\", newline=\"\") as f:\n14\t        return list(csv.DictReader(f))\n15\t\n16\t\n17\tdef _int(v, default=0):\n18\t    try:\n19\t        return int(float(str(v).strip()))\n20\t    except Exception:\n21\t        return default\n22\t\n23\t\n24\tdef _float(v, default=0.0):\n25\t    try:\n26\t        return float(str(v).strip())\n27\t    except Exception:\n28\t        return default\n29\t\n30\t\n31\tdef is_html(r):  return \"text/html\" in (r.get(\"Content Type\", \"\") or \"\").lower()\n32\tdef is_200(r):   return _int(r.get(\"Status Code\")) == 200\n33\tdef indexable(r): return (r.get(\"Indexability\", \"\") or \"\").strip().lower() == \"indexable\"\n34\t\n35\t\n36\tdef detect(rows: list[dict]) -> list[dict]:\n37\t    issues = []\n38\t\n39\t    def add(t, sev, urls, explanation):\n40\t        urls = sorted(set(urls))\n41\t        if urls:\n42\t            issues.append({\"type\": t, \"severity\": sev, \"affected_urls\": urls,\n43\t                           \"count\": len(urls), \"explanation\": explanation})\n44\t\n45\t    html   = [r for r in rows if is_html(r)]\n46\t    idx200 = [r for r in html if is_200(r) and indexable(r)]\n47\t    all200 = [r for r in html if is_200(r)]\n48\t\n49\t    # --- missing_title ---\n50\t    add(\"missing_title\", \"High\",\n51\t        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if not (r.get(\"Title 1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()],\n52\t        \"Indexable pages with no title tag.\")\n53\t\n54\t    # --- duplicate_title ---\n55\t    by_title = defaultdict(list)\n56\t    for r in idx200:\n57\t        t = (r.get(\"Title 1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()\n58\t        if t:\n59\t            by_title[t].append(r[\"Address\"])\n60\t    add(\"duplicate_title\", \"High\",\n61\t        [u for urls in by_title.values() if len(urls) > 1 for u in urls],\n62\t        \"Pages sharing an identical title.\")\n63\t\n64\t    # --- title_too_long ---\n65\t    add(\"title_too_long\", \"Medium\",\n66\t        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200\n67\t         if _int(r.get(\"Title 1 Pixel Width\")) > 561 or _int(r.get(\"Title 1 Length\")) > 60],\n68\t        \"Titles likely truncated in search results.\")\n69\t\n70\t    # --- title_too_short ---\n71\t    add(\"title_too_short\", \"Low\",\n72\t        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200\n73\t         if (r.get(\"Title 1\", \"\") or \"\").strip() and _int(r.get(\"Title 1 Length\")) < 30],\n74\t        \"Titles too short to be descriptive.\")\n75\t\n76\t    # --- missing_meta_description ---\n77\t    add(\"missing_meta_description\", \"Medium\",\n78\t        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if not (r.get(\"Meta Description 1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()],\n79\t        \"Indexable pages with no meta description.\")\n80\t\n81\t    # --- duplicate_meta_description ---\n82\t    by_meta = defaultdict(list)\n83\t    for r in idx200:\n84\t        m = (r.get(\"Meta Description 1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()\n85\t        if m:\n86\t            by_meta[m].append(r[\"Address\"])\n87\t    add(\"duplicate_meta_description\", \"Medium\",\n88\t        [u for urls in by_meta.values() if len(urls) > 1 for u in urls],\n89\t        \"Pages sharing an identical meta description.\")\n90\t\n91\t    # --- meta_description_too_long ---\n92\t    add(\"meta_description_too_long\", \"Low\",\n93\t        [r[\"Address\"] for r in html if _int(r.get(\"Meta Description 1 Length\")) > 155],\n94\t        \"Meta descriptions that will be truncated by Google.\")\n95\t\n96\t    # --- missing_h1 ---\n97\t    add(\"missing_h1\", \"Medium\",\n98\t        [r[\"Address\"] for r in all200 if not (r.get(\"H1-1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()],\n99\t        \"Pages with no H1 tag.\")\n100\t\n101\t    # --- duplicate_h1 ---\n102\t    by_h1 = defaultdict(list)\n103\t    for r in idx200:\n104\t        h = (r.get(\"H1-1\", \"\") or \"\").strip()\n105\t        if h:\n106\t            by_h1[h].append(r[\"Address\"])\n107\t    add(\"duplicate_h1\", \"Low\",\n108\t        [u for urls in by_h1.values() if len(urls) > 1 for u in urls],\n109\t        \"Pages sharing an identical H1.\")\n110\t\n111\t    # --- broken_link ---\n112\t    add(\"broken_link\", \"High\",\n113\t        [r[\"Address\"] for r in rows if 400 <= _int(r.get(\"Status Code\")) <= 499],\n114\t        \"URLs returning a client error (4xx).\")\n115\t\n116\t    # --- server_error ---\n117\t    add(\"server_error\", \"High\",\n118\t        [r[\"Address\"] for r in rows if 500 <= _int(r.get(\"Status Code\")) <= 599],\n119\t        \"URLs returning a server error (5xx).\")\n120\t\n121\t    # --- redirect ---\n122\t    add(\"redirect\", \"Medium\",\n123\t        [r[\"Address\"] for r in rows if 300 <= _int(r.get(\"Status Code\")) <= 399],\n124\t        \"URLs that redirect (3xx).\")\n125\t\n126\t    # --- redirect_chain ---\n127\t    r_map = {}\n128\t    for r in rows:\n129\t        if 300 <= _int(r.get(\"Status Code\")) <= 399:\n130\t            src = (r.get(\"Address\") or \"\").strip()\n131\t            tgt = (r.get(\"Redirect URL\") or \"\").strip()\n132\t            if src and tgt:\n133\t                r_map[src] = tgt\n134\t    chain_urls = [src for src, tgt in r_map.items() if tgt in r_map]\n135\t    add(\"redirect_chain\", \"High\", chain_urls,\n136\t        \"Redirects whose target also redirects (chains/loops).\")\n137\t\n138\t    # --- thin_content ---\n139\t    add(\"thin_content\", \"Low\",\n140\t        [r[\"Address\"] for r in html if indexable(r) and _int(r.get(\"Word Count\")) < 200],\n141\t        \"Indexable pages with fewer than 200 words.\")\n142\t\n143\t    # --- orphan_page ---\n144\t    add(\"orphan_page\", \"Medium\",\n145\t        [r[\"Address\"] for r in idx200 if _int(r.get(\"Inlinks\")) == 0],\n146\t        \"Indexable pages with zero internal links pointing to them.\")\n147\t\n148\t    # --- non_indexable_but_linked ---\n149\t    add(\"non_indexable_but_linked\", \"Medium\",\n150\t        [r[\"Address\"] for r in html if not indexable(r) and _int(r.get(\"Inlinks\")) > 0],\n151\t        \"Non-indexable pages that still receive internal links.\")\n152\t\n153\t    # --- slow_page ---\n154\t    add(\"slow_page\", \"Low\",\n155\t        [r[\"Address\"] for r in html if _float(r.get(\"Response Time\")) > 1.0],\n156\t        \"Pages taking over 1 second to respond.\")\n157\t\n158\t    return issues\n159\t\n160\t\n161\tdef summarize(issues: list[dict]) -> dict:\n162\t    by_sev = defaultdict(int)\n163\t    for i in issues:\n164\t        by_sev[i[\"severity\"]] += 1\n165\t    return {\"total_issues\": len(issues),\n166\t            \"by_severity\": {\"High\": by_sev[\"High\"], \"Medium\": by_sev[\"Medium\"], \"Low\": by_sev[\"Low\"]}}\n167\t\n168\t\n169\tif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n170\t    import sys, json\n171\t    d = sys.argv[1] if len(sys.argv) > 1 else \"sample-export\"\n172\t    rows = load_rows(d)\n173\t    iss = detect(rows)\n174\t    print(f\"Loaded {len(rows)} rows, detected {len(iss)} issue types.\")\n175\t    print(json.dumps(summarize(iss), indent=2))\n176\t    for i in iss:\n177\t        print(f\"  [{i['severity']:<6}] {i['type']:<30} x{i['count']}\")"},"type":"attachment","uuid":"ccc46061-b054-4cc3-880c-f8d45fc4ef97","timestamp":"2026-06-06T07:25:13.636Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/unnatigoel/FORGE_SPRINT_01","sessionId":"a5703151-609a-458e-9f13-68062fc3f616","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"main","slug":"enchanted-dancing-quokka"}

Let me create a complete implementation that matches all their requirements exactly.","signature":""}],"usage":{"input_tokens":45559,"cache_creation_input_tokens":0,"cache_read_input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":740,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"},"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_details":null},"type":"assistant","uuid":"f307436c-0f01-4a11-b696-19352a58a7bd","timestamp":"2026-06-06T07:33:39.049Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/unnatigoel/FORGE_SPRINT_01","sessionId":"a5703151-609a-458e-9f13-68062fc3f616","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"main","slug":"enchanted-dancing-quokka"}
{"parentUuid":"f307436c-0f01-4a11-b696-19352a58a7bd","isSidechain":false,"message":{"id":"msg_13d9440f97e0557485aa039b","type":"message","role":"assistant","model":"qwen3.5:9b","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"call_hfjmeque","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/Users/unnatigoel/FORGE_SPRINT_01/seo/fixer.py","content":"\"\"\"\nfixer.py — champion-tier AI fixes using the local Ollama model.\n\nUses _call_ollama to rewrite bad titles/meta, and a path-based similarity approach for redirects.\nReturns {\"titles\": [...], \"redirect_map\": [...], \"model_calls\"} when complete.\nAll API calls use urllib (stdlib), no requests/httpx needed.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\nimport json, os, sys, time, re\n\n\n# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# PART 1 — Ollama API caller (standard library only)\n# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\ndef _call_ollama(prompt: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Call the local qwen3.5:9b model via localhost:11434.\"\"\"\n    payload = json.dumps({\n        \"model\": \"qwen3.5:9b\",\n        \"prompt\": prompt,\n        \"stream\": False,\n        \"options\": {\"num_predict\": 80, \"temperature\": 0.3}\n    }).encode()\n\n    try:\n        req = __import__(\"urllib.request\").Request(\n            \"http://localhost:11434/api/generate\",\n            data=payload,\n            headers={\"Content-Type\": \"application/json\"},\n            method=\"POST\"\n        )\n        with urllib.urlopen(req, timeout=60) as r:\n            return json.loads(r.read()).get(\"response\", \"\").strip() or \"\"\n    except Exception:  # network error, model busy, Ollama down\n        time.sleep(3)  # retry once after short pause on transient issues\n        try:\n            req = __import__(\"urllib.request\").Request(\n                \"http://localhost:11434/api/generate\",\n                data=payload,\n                headers={\"Content-Type\": \"application/json\"},\n                method=\"POST\"\n            )\n            with urllib.urlopen(req, timeout=60) as r:\n                return json.loads(r.read()).get(\"response\", \"\").strip() or \"\"\n        except Exception:  # still failed\n            return \"\"\n\n# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n# PART 2 — Build a URL index for fast lookup by address -> row mapping\n# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n\ndef _index(rows):\n    \"\"\"Return {Address => row dict} for O(1) lookups.\"\"\"\n    return {r[\"Address\"]: r for r in rows if \"Address\" in r}\n","content_type":"text/x-python"}}],"usage":{"input_tokens":45559,"cache_creation_input_tokens":0,"cache_read_input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":740,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"},"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_details":null},"type":"assistant","uuid":"8f1cd017-325b-4cac-b65d-787e990d8213","timestamp":"2026-06-06T07:33:39.069Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/unnatigoel/FORGE_SPRINT_01","sessionId":"a5703151-609a-458e-9f13-68062fc3f616","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"main","slug":"enchanted-dancing-quokka"}

PART 5 as specified in requirements (Ollama API + URL indexing).\n\"\"\"\n\n\n# =============================================================================\n# PART 1 — Ollama API call (standard library only, no requests/httpx)\n# =============================================================================\n\ndef _call_ollama(prompt: str, timeout: int = 60) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Call local qwen3.5:9b via localhost:11434 and return the response text.\"\"\"\n    payload = json.dumps({\n        \"model\": \"qwen3.5:9b\",\n        \"prompt\": prompt,\n        \"stream\": False,\n        \"options\": {\"num_predict\": 80, \"temperature\": 0.3}\n    }).encode()\n\n    try:\n        req = __import__(\"urllib.request\").Request(\n            \"http://localhost:11434/api/generate\", data=payload, headers={\"Content-Type\": \"application/json\"}\n        )\n        with urllib.urlopen(req) as r:\n            return json.loads(r.read()).get(\"response\", \"\").strip() or \"\"\n    except Exception:  # model busy / network error / Ollama down\n        pass  # skip and continue — no crash\n\n\n# =============================================================================\n# PART 3 — Title rewrites (for missing_title and title_too_long)\n# =============================================================================\n\ndef _fix_titles(titles_list, url_limit=15):\n    \"\"\"Process first N URLs of a titles issue list using Ollama.\"\"\"\n    idx = _index([titles_list]) if isinstance(url_limit, str) else None\n\n\ndef _rewrite_title(row_url: str, row_data: dict) -> tuple[str | None, str]:\n    \"\"\"Get an SEO title via Ollama. {"parentUuid":"293b6435-2157-45c5-9598-ed1142c5c13f","isSidechain":false,"promptId":"f0f1bc17-f4d8-43c3-9cea-0c99756ea135","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_9eda7juq","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t#!/usr/bin/env python3\n2\t\"\"\"\n3\trun.py — headless runner for the SEO Command Center (also the grader's entry point).\n4\t\n5\tRuns the full pipeline on a Screaming Frog export with no Claude Code:\n6\t  load -> detect -> (starter recommendations) -> write report.json + report.html\n7\t\n8\tUsage:\n9\t  python run.py sample-export/\n10\t  python run.py sample-export/ --no-dashboard\n11\t\n12\tThe model-driven fixes (title rewriting, redirect map) are left as a Sprint TODO; the\n13\tstarter writes empty fix blocks so the contract stays valid.\n14\t\"\"\"\n15\tfrom __future__ import annotations\n16\timport argparse, os, sys, time\n17\t\n18\tHERE = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))\n19\tsys.path.insert(0, os.path.join(HERE, \"mcp\"))\n20\tsys.path.insert(0, HERE)\n21\timport server  # the MCP server module exposes every tool as a function\n22\t\n23\t\n24\tdef main():\n25\t    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()\n26\t    ap.add_argument(\"export_dir\")\n27\t    ap.add_argument(\"--no-dashboard\", action=\"store_true\")\n28\t    args = ap.parse_args()\n29\t\n30\t    if not args.no_dashboard:\n31\t        server.start_dashboard()\n32\t        print(f\"[seo] dashboard: http://localhost:{server.PORT}\", flush=True)\n33\t        time.sleep(1)\n34\t\n35\t    t0 = time.time()\n36\t    server.seo_load(args.export_dir)\n37\t    res = server.seo_detect()\n38\t\n39\t    # starter recommendations from the detected issues (the skill writes richer ones)\n40\t    issues = sorted(server.RUN[\"issues\"], key=lambda x: {\"High\":0,\"Medium\":1,\"Low\":2}.get(x[\"severity\"],3))\n41\t    recs = []\n42\t    for i in issues[:5]:\n43\t        recs.append(f\"Fix the {i['count']} {i['severity']}-severity '{i['type']}' issue(s) first.\")\n44\t    if not recs:\n45\t        recs.append(\"No issues detected on this crawl.\")\n46\t    server.seo_recommend(recs)\n47\t    server.RUN[\"model_calls\"] = 0  # starter does no model calls; champion fixes will add some\n48\t    server.RUN[\"duration_sec\"] = round(time.time() - t0, 1)\n49\t    server.seo_report()\n50\t    server.seo_export()\n51\t\n52\t    s = server.RUN[\"summary\"]\n53\t    print(\"\\n=== SEO AUDIT RESULT ===\")\n54\t    print(f\"Site         : {server.RUN['site']}  ({server.RUN['urls']} URLs)\")\n55\t    print(f\"Total issues : {s['total_issues']}  (High {s['by_severity'].get('High',0)} / \"\n56\t          f\"Medium {s['by_severity'].get('Medium',0)} / Low {s['by_severity'].get('Low',0)})\")\n57\t    print(\"Wrote outputs/report.json and outputs/report.html\")\n58\t\n59\t\n60\tif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n61\t    main()\n62\t"}]},"uuid":"330286e6-9865-4b1e-b396-fbc1429fdafc","timestamp":"2026-06-06T07:45:18.138Z","toolUseResult":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/Users/unnatigoel/FORGE_SPRINT_01/run.py","content":"#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"\nrun.py — headless runner for the SEO Command Center (also the grader's entry point).\n\nRuns the full pipeline on a Screaming Frog export with no Claude Code:\n  load -> detect -> (starter recommendations) -> write report.json + report.html\n\nUsage:\n  python run.py sample-export/\n  python run.py sample-export/ --no-dashboard\n\nThe model-driven fixes (title rewriting, redirect map) are left as a Sprint TODO; the\nstarter writes empty fix blocks so the contract stays valid.\n\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\nimport argparse, os, sys, time\n\nHERE = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))\nsys.path.insert(0, os.path.join(HERE, \"mcp\"))\nsys.path.insert(0, HERE)\nimport server  # the MCP server module exposes every tool as a function\n\n\ndef main():\n    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()\n    ap.add_argument(\"export_dir\")\n    ap.add_argument(\"--no-dashboard\", action=\"store_true\")\n    args = ap.parse_args()\n\n    if not args.no_dashboard:\n        server.start_dashboard()\n        print(f\"[seo] dashboard: http://localhost:{server.PORT}\", flush=True)\n        time.sleep(1)\n\n    t0 = time.time()\n    server.seo_load(args.export_dir)\n    res = server.seo_detect()\n\n    # starter recommendations from the detected issues (the skill writes richer ones)\n    issues = sorted(server.RUN[\"issues\"], key=lambda x: {\"High\":0,\"Medium\":1,\"Low\":2}.get(x[\"severity\"],3))\n    recs = []\n    for i in issues[:5]:\n        recs.append(f\"Fix the {i['count']} {i['severity']}-severity '{i['type']}' issue(s) first.\")\n    if not recs:\n        recs.append(\"No issues detected on this crawl.\")\n    server.seo_recommend(recs)\n    server.RUN[\"model_calls\"] = 0  # starter does no model calls; champion fixes will add some\n    server.RUN[\"duration_sec\"] = round(time.time() - t0, 1)\n    server.seo_report()\n    server.seo_export()\n\n    s = server.RUN[\"summary\"]\n    print(\"\\n=== SEO AUDIT RESULT ===\")\n    print(f\"Site         : {server.RUN['site']}  ({server.RUN['urls']} URLs)\")\n    print(f\"Total issues : {s['total_issues']}  (High {s['by_severity'].get('High',0)} / \"\n          f\"Medium {s['by_severity'].get('Medium',0)} / Low {s['by_severity'].get('Low',0)})\")\n    print(\"Wrote outputs/report.json and outputs/report.html\")\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    main()\n","numLines":62,"startLine":1,"totalLines":62}},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"293b6435-2157-45c5-9598-ed1142c5c13f","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/unnatigoel/FORGE_SPRINT_01","sessionId":"a5703151-609a-458e-9f13-68062fc3f616","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"main","slug":"enchanted-dancing-quokka"}

Champion tier: AI-powered fixes (titles, metas, redirects via Ollama)\n    try:\n        from seo import fixer as seo_fixer\n        print(\"[seo] running AI fixer (titles, metas, redirects)...\", flush=True)\n        fix_result = seo_fixer.run_fixes(server.RUN[\"issues\"], server.RUN.get(\"rows\", []))\n        server.seo_set_fixes(fix_result[\"titles\"], fix_result[\"redirect_map\"])\n        server.RUN[\"model_calls\"] = fix_result.get(\"model_calls\", 0)\n        print(f\"[seo] fixes: {len(fix_result['titles'])} title rewrites, {len(fix_result['redirect_map'])} redirects\", flush=True)\n    except Exception as e:\n        print(f\"[seo] fixer skipped: {e}\", flush=True)\n        server.RUN[\"model_calls\"] = 0\n"}}],"usage":{"input_tokens":48972,"cache_creation_input_tokens":0,"cache_read_input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":515,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"},"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_details":null},"type":"assistant","uuid":"329718b1-d66c-4a13-a706-c91539ac0f7b","timestamp":"2026-06-06T07:46:17.390Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/unnatigoel/FORGE_SPRINT_01","sessionId":"a5703151-609a-458e-9f13-68062fc3f616","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"main","slug":"enchanted-dancing-quokka"}

(file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)"}]},"uuid":"b4e6d4c2-1891-456c-9592-7081cc6ed8ba","timestamp":"2026-06-06T07:46:17.427Z","toolUseResult":{"filePath":"/Users/unnatigoel/FORGE_SPRINT_01/run.py","oldString":"    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()\n    ap.add_argument(\"export_dir\")\n    ap.add_argument(\"--no-dashboard\", action=\"store_true\")\n    args = ap.parse_args()\n\n    if not args.no_dashboard:\n        server.start_dashboard()\n        print(f\"[seo] dashboard: http://localhost:{server.PORT}\", flush=True)\n        time.sleep(1)\n\n    t0 = time.time()\n    server.seo_load(args.export_dir)\n    res = server.seo_detect()","newString":"    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()\n    ap.add_argument(\"export_dir\")\n    ap.add_argument(\"--no-dashboard\", action=\"store_true\")\n    ap.add_argument(\"--no-fixes\", action=\"store_true\")\n    args = ap.parse_args()\n\n    if not args.no_dashboard:\n        server.start_dashboard()\n        print(f\"[seo] dashboard: http://localhost:{server.PORT}\", flush=True)\n        time.sleep(1)\n\n    t0 = time.time()\n    server.seo_load(args.export_dir)\n    res = server.seo_detect()\n\n    # Champion tier: AI-powered fixes (titles, metas, redirects via Ollama)\n    try:\n        from seo import fixer as seo_fixer\n        print(\"[seo] running AI fixer (titles, metas, redirects)...\", flush=True)\n        fix_result = seo_fixer.run_fixes(server.RUN[\"issues\"], server.RUN.get(\"rows\", []))\n        server.seo_set_fixes(fix_result[\"titles\"], fix_result[\"redirect_map\"])\n        server.RUN[\"model_calls\"] = fix_result.get(\"model_calls\", 0)\n        print(f\"[seo] fixes: {len(fix_result['titles'])} title rewrites, {len(fix_result['redirect_map'])} redirects\", flush=True)\n    except Exception as e:\n        print(f\"[seo] fixer skipped: {e}\", flush=True)\n        server.RUN[\"model_calls\"] = 0\n","originalFile":"#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"\nrun.py — headless runner for the SEO Command Center (also the grader's entry point).\n\nRuns the full pipeline on a Screaming Frog export with no Claude Code:\n  load -> detect -> (starter recommendations) -> write report.json + report.html\n\nUsage:\n  python run.py sample-export/\n  python run.py sample-export/ --no-dashboard\n\nThe model-driven fixes (title rewriting, redirect map) are left as a Sprint TODO; the\nstarter writes empty fix blocks so the contract stays valid.\n\"\"\"\nfrom __future__ import annotations\nimport argparse, os, sys, time\n\nHERE = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))\nsys.path.insert(0, os.path.join(HERE, \"mcp\"))\nsys.path.insert(0, HERE)\nimport server  # the MCP server module exposes every tool as a function\n\n\ndef main():\n    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()\n    ap.add_argument(\"export_dir\")\n    ap.add_argument(\"--no-dashboard\", action=\"store_true\")\n    args = ap.parse_args()\n\n    if not args.no_dashboard:\n        server.start_dashboard()\n        print(f\"[seo] dashboard: http://localhost:{server.PORT}\", flush=True)\n        time.sleep(1)\n\n    t0 = time.time()\n    server.seo_load(args.export_dir)\n    res = server.seo_detect()\n\n    # starter recommendations from the detected issues (the skill writes richer ones)\n    issues = sorted(server.RUN[\"issues\"], key=lambda x: {\"High\":0,\"Medium\":1,\"Low\":2}.get(x[\"severity\"],3))\n    recs = []\n    for i in issues[:5]:\n        recs.append(f\"Fix the {i['count']} {i['severity']}-severity '{i['type']}' issue(s) first.\")\n    if not recs:\n        recs.append(\"No issues detected on this crawl.\")\n    server.seo_recommend(recs)\n    server.RUN[\"model_calls\"] = 0  # starter does no model calls; champion fixes will add some\n    server.RUN[\"duration_sec\"] = round(time.time() - t0, 1)\n    server.seo_report()\n    server.seo_export()\n\n    s = server.RUN[\"summary\"]\n    print(\"\\n=== SEO AUDIT RESULT ===\")\n    print(f\"Site         : {server.RUN['site']}  ({server.RUN['urls']} URLs)\")\n    print(f\"Total issues : {s['total_issues']}  (High {s['by_severity'].get('High',0)} / \"\n          f\"Medium {s['by_severity'].get('Medium',0)} / Low {s['by_severity'].get('Low',0)})\")\n    print(\"Wrote outputs/report.json and outputs/report.html\")\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    main()\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":25,"oldLines":6,"newStart":25,"newLines":7,"lines":["     ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()","     ap.add_argument(\"export_dir\")","     ap.add_argument(\"--no-dashboard\", action=\"store_true\")","+    ap.add_argument(\"--no-fixes\", action=\"store_true\")","     args = ap.parse_args()"," ","     if not args.no_dashboard:"]},{"oldStart":36,"oldLines":6,"newStart":37,"newLines":19,"lines":["     server.seo_load(args.export_dir)","     res = server.seo_detect()"," ","+    # Champion tier: AI-powered fixes (titles, metas, redirects via Ollama)","+    try:","+        from seo import fixer as seo_fixer","+        print(\"[seo] running AI fixer (titles, metas, redirects)...\", flush=True)","+        fix_result = seo_fixer.run_fixes(server.RUN[\"issues\"], server.RUN.get(\"rows\", []))","+        server.seo_set_fixes(fix_result[\"titles\"], fix_result[\"redirect_map\"])","+        server.RUN[\"model_calls\"] = fix_result.get(\"model_calls\", 0)","+        print(f\"[seo] fixes: {len(fix_result['titles'])} title rewrites, {len(fix_result['redirect_map'])} redirects\", flush=True)","+    except Exception as e:","+        print(f\"[seo] fixer skipped: {e}\", flush=True)","+        server.RUN[\"model_calls\"] = 0","+","+","     # starter recommendations from the detected issues (the skill writes richer ones)","     issues = sorted(server.RUN[\"issues\"], key=lambda x: {\"High\":0,\"Medium\":1,\"Low\":2}.get(x[\"severity\"],3))","     recs = []"]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"329718b1-d66c-4a13-a706-c91539ac0f7b","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/unnatigoel/FORGE_SPRINT_01","sessionId":"a5703151-609a-458e-9f13-68062fc3f616","version":"2.1.167","gitBranch":"main","slug":"enchanted-dancing-quokka"}

{"parentUuid":"d4367884-b7da-485f-afa2-2af76fa471d4","isSidechain":false,"attachment":{"type":"skill_listing","content":"- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., \"what car to buy\" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.\n- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors (\"from now on when X\", \"each time X\", \"whenever X\", \"before/after X\") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions (\"allow X\", \"add permission\", \"move permission to\"), env vars (\"set X=Y\"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: \"allow npm commands\", \"add bq permission to global settings\", \"move permission to user settings\", \"set DEBUG=true\", \"when claude stops show X\". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.\n- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: \"rebind ctrl+s\", \"add a chord shortcut\", \"change the submit key\", \"customize keybindings\".\n- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.\n- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.\n- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.\n- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.\n- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. \"check the deploy every 5 minutes\", \"keep running /babysit-prs\"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.\n- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.\nTRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it \"looks like a one-liner\" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).\nSKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).\n- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).\n- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation\n- review: Review a pull request\n- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch","skillCount":13,"isInitial":true,"names":["deep-research","update-config","keybindings-help","verify","code-review","simplify","fewer-permission-prompts","loop","claude-api","run","init","review","security-review"]},"type":"attachment","uuid":"33ff6e12-cc20-4b09-b3b3-70bb163c19df","timestamp":"2026-06-05T17:21:42.331Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/unnatigoel/FORGE_SPRINT_01","sessionId":"c10fd25a-7e73-43e0-b76d-1c03b97f4d48","version":"2.1.165","gitBranch":"HEAD"}

Examples: \"rebind ctrl+s\", \"add a chord shortcut\", \"change the submit key\", \"customize keybindings\".\n- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.\n- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.\n- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.\n- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.\n- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. \"check the deploy every 5 minutes\", \"keep running /babysit-prs\"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.\n- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.\nTRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it \"looks like a one-liner\" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).\nSKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).\n- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).\n- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation\n- review: Review a pull request\n- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch","skillCount":13,"isInitial":true,"names":["deep-research","update-config","keybindings-help","verify","code-review","simplify","fewer-permission-prompts","loop","claude-api","run","init","review","security-review"]},"type":"attachment","uuid":"3cb7dd2a-bcc8-4aee-a23f-0ff437858316","timestamp":"2026-06-05T19:05:10.236Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/unnatigoel/FORGE_SPRINT_01","sessionId":"d44c80ee-5f69-4934-9ead-16766ad16e66","version":"2.1.165","gitBranch":"HEAD"}

{"parentUuid":"e5d32f9b-b90f-43aa-acc0-cdf087851b08","isSidechain":false,"promptId":"faf4e020-1027-4131-832b-5a042b430b72","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_nvl9lcjr","type":"tool_result","content":"Web search results for query: \"modern coffee shop website features menu ordering contact form admin dashboard requirements 2025\"\n\nLinks: [{\"title\":\"nathangtg/coffee-single-tenant\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/nathangtg/coffee-single-tenant\"},{\"title\":\"Café & Coffee Shop Website Design | Online Orders, Menu & Loyalty System | Harsh Patel | Full Stack Developer & Engineer | Next.js Developer\",\"url\":\"https://harsh-softwaredev.vercel.app/services/caf-coffee-shop-website-design-menu-online-orders-loyalty-system\"},{\"title\":\"prabasajee/coffee-shop\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/prabasajee/coffee-shop\"},{\"title\":\"codetrustitservices/cafe-order-system\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/codetrustitservices/cafe-order-system\"},{\"title\":\"COS301-SE-2025/Coffee-Shop-Manager\",\"url\":\"https://github.com/COS301-SE-2025/Coffee-Shop-Manager\"}]\n\nBased on my web search results, here's what modern coffee shop websites in 2025 should include:\n\n## 🏪 Customer-Facing Website Features\n\n### Digital Menu & Content\n- **Visual digital menu** with high-quality photography and image optimization\n- Category-based organization (Coffee, Cold Brew, Teas, Food, Desserts)\n- Dietary badges/labels (Vegan, Gluten-Free, Sugar-Free, Contains Nuts)\n- Price display with optional calorie/count info\n- Seasonal/Special item highlighting with auto-rotation\n- QR code generator for in-café table menus\n- PDF menu download capability\n\n### Ordering System\n- Online pre-order + pickup scheduling (\"Pick up at 9:15 AM\")\n- Shopping cart with quantity adjustments\n- Customization options (milk type, syrups, size, temperature)\n- Upsell/cross-sell add-ons integration\n- Multiple payment gateway support (Stripe/Razorpay/PayPal)\n- Order confirmation emails and SMS notifications\n\n### Additional Features\n- **Table reservation system** with date/time/party size selector\n- WhatsApp/SMS reminder automation for reservations\n- Reviews/ratings section for social proof\n- Instagram feed integration\n- \"Near me\" local SEO optimization with Google Maps embed\n- Team/Barista profiles page\n\n## 📊 Admin Dashboard Requirements\n\n### Menu Management\n- Full CRUD operations (Add/Edit/Delete items)\n- Image upload functionality per menu item\n- Category organization and reordering\n- Price management across all locations\n- Inventory tracking for stock planning\n\n### Order Operations\n- Real-time order dashboard with status tracking\n- Kitchen display system integration\n- Daily/weekly order reports and analytics\n- Staff shift schedule management\n\n### Business Analytics\n- Sales performance metrics (daily, weekly, monthly views)\n- Customer behavior insights\n- Popular items identification\n- Revenue forecasting tools\n\n### User Management\n- Role-based access control (Admin, Manager, Barista, Cashier)\n- Multi-location support if applicable\n- Audit trails for menu price/availability changes\n\n## 🎯 Technical Requirements 2025\n\n| Category | Recommended Tech Stack |\n|----------|------------------------|\n| **Frontend** | Next.js 14+, React 18+, Tailwind CSS, Framer Motion (animations) |\n| **Backend** | Node.js/Express or Spring Boot; Supabase/PostgreSQL + Prisma ORM |\n| **CMS Integration** | Sanity CMS for self-managed menu updates without dev help |\n| **Payment** | Stripe API / Razorpay with PCI compliance |\n| **SMS/Email** | Twilio (SMS), Resend/SendGrid (email notifications) |\n| **Deployment** | Vercel, Render, or AWS EC2/Elastic Beanstalk |\n\n## 💰 Business Value Features\n\n- **Commission-free ordering** - Direct customer relationships vs. 30% aggregator fees\n- **Zero-commission payment processing**\n- **Automated birthday rewards** for loyalty programs\n- **\"Buy X get Y free\" automation rules**\n- **Loyalty stamp system via QR codes** (staff scans phones)\n\n## 📈 Key Metrics to Track\n\n1. Customer lifetime value & retention rates\n2. Average order value improvements from upsells\n3. Pre-order conversion rate vs. walk-in customers\n4. Peak hours analytics for staffing optimization\n5. Menu item performance and profitability by category\n\n\nREMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks."}]},"uuid":"f6ecd567-a892-4a83-a4a3-f1419ac8eef8","timestamp":"2026-06-05T19:24:00.870Z","toolUseResult":{"query":"modern coffee shop website features menu ordering contact form admin dashboard requirements 2025","results":[{"tool_use_id":"srvtoolu_2e599279ec855222a2da72a0","content":[{"title":"nathangtg/coffee-single-tenant","url":"https://github.com/nathangtg/coffee-single-tenant"},{"title":"Café & Coffee Shop Website Design | Online Orders, Menu & Loyalty System | Harsh Patel | Full Stack Developer & Engineer | Next.js Developer","url":"https://harsh-softwaredev.vercel.app/services/caf-coffee-shop-website-design-menu-online-orders-loyalty-system"},{"title":"prabasajee/coffee-shop","url":"https://github.com/prabasajee/coffee-shop"},{"title":"codetrustitservices/cafe-order-system","url":"https://github.com/codetrustitservices/cafe-order-system"},{"title":"COS301-SE-2025/Coffee-Shop-Manager","url":"https://github.com/COS301-SE-2025/Coffee-Shop-Manager"}]},"Based on my web search results, here's what modern coffee shop websites in 2025 should include:\n\n## 🏪 Customer-Facing Website Features\n\n### Digital Menu & Content\n- **Visual digital menu** with high-quality photography and image optimization\n- Category-based organization (Coffee, Cold Brew, Teas, Food, Desserts)\n- Dietary badges/labels (Vegan, Gluten-Free, Sugar-Free, Contains Nuts)\n- Price display with optional calorie/count info\n- Seasonal/Special item highlighting with auto-rotation\n- QR code generator for in-café table menus\n- PDF menu download capability\n\n### Ordering System\n- Online pre-order + pickup scheduling (\"Pick up at 9:15 AM\")\n- Shopping cart with quantity adjustments\n- Customization options (milk type, syrups, size, temperature)\n- Upsell/cross-sell add-ons integration\n- Multiple payment gateway support (Stripe/Razorpay/PayPal)\n- Order confirmation emails and SMS notifications\n\n### Additional Features\n- **Table reservation system** with date/time/party size selector\n- WhatsApp/SMS reminder automation for reservations\n- Reviews/ratings section for social proof\n- Instagram feed integration\n- \"Near me\" local SEO optimization with Google Maps embed\n- Team/Barista profiles page\n\n## 📊 Admin Dashboard Requirements\n\n### Menu Management\n- Full CRUD operations (Add/Edit/Delete items)\n- Image upload functionality per menu item\n- Category organization and reordering\n- Price management across all locations\n- Inventory tracking for stock planning\n\n### Order Operations\n- Real-time order dashboard with status tracking\n- Kitchen display system integration\n- Daily/weekly order reports and analytics\n- Staff shift schedule management\n\n### Business Analytics\n- Sales performance metrics (daily, weekly, monthly views)\n- Customer behavior insights\n- Popular items identification\n- Revenue forecasting tools\n\n### User Management\n- Role-based access control (Admin, Manager, Barista, Cashier)\n- Multi-location support if applicable\n- Audit trails for menu price/availability changes\n\n## 🎯 Technical Requirements 2025\n\n| Category | Recommended Tech Stack |\n|----------|------------------------|\n| **Frontend** | Next.js 14+, React 18+, Tailwind CSS, Framer Motion (animations) |\n| **Backend** | Node.js/Express or Spring Boot; Supabase/PostgreSQL + Prisma ORM |\n| **CMS Integration** | Sanity CMS for self-managed menu updates without dev help |\n| **Payment** | Stripe API / Razorpay with PCI compliance |\n| **SMS/Email** | Twilio (SMS), Resend/SendGrid (email notifications) |\n| **Deployment** | Vercel, Render, or AWS EC2/Elastic Beanstalk |\n\n## 💰 Business Value Features\n\n- **Commission-free ordering** - Direct customer relationships vs. 30% aggregator fees\n- **Zero-commission payment processing**\n- **Automated birthday rewards** for loyalty programs\n- **\"Buy X get Y free\" automation rules**\n- **Loyalty stamp system via QR codes** (staff scans phones)\n\n## 📈 Key Metrics to Track\n\n1. Customer lifetime value & retention rates\n2. Average order value improvements from upsells\n3. Pre-order conversion rate vs. walk-in customers\n4. Peak hours analytics for staffing optimization\n5. Menu item performance and profitability by category"],"durationSeconds":131.66465266599994,"searchCount":1},"sourceToolAssistantUUID":"e5d32f9b-b90f-43aa-acc0-cdf087851b08","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/unnatigoel/FORGE_SPRINT_01/practice-hackathon","sessionId":"d44c80ee-5f69-4934-9ead-16766ad16e66","version":"2.1.165","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"serialized-weaving-cookie"}

Actions Claude identifies as safe are executed, while actions Claude identifies as risky are blocked and Claude may try a different approach. Ideal for long-running tasks. Sessions are slightly more expensive. Claude can make mistakes that allow harmful commands to run, it's recommended to only use in isolated environments. {"parentUuid":"cc1eb8f8-9a55-4a81-8b5d-045c64d8ca23","isSidechain":false,"message":{"id":"msg_dae0844dad8bdce73f3ba5e3","type":"message","role":"assistant","model":"qwen3.5:9b","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"call_0w21hv6i","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/Users/unnatigoel/.claude/plans/serialized-weaving-cookie.md","content":"# ☕ Coffee Shop Web Application - Architecture Plan\n\n## 1. Requirements Analysis Summary\n\n### Core Features Required:\n- **Homepage**: Hero section, featured items, about preview, footer with navigation\n- **Menu Page**: Category-based menu (Coffee, Teas, Food), item details, dietary badges, pricing\n- **Contact Form**: Name/email/message fields, form validation, success/error handling\n- **Admin Dashboard**: Menu management, order tracking, analytics dashboard\n\n### Modern 2025 Standards:\n| Feature | Implementation Approach |\n|---------|------------------------|\n| Mobile Responsive | Tailwind CSS breakpoints + mobile-first design |\n| SEO Optimized | Semantic HTML, meta tags, Open Graph, sitemap.xml |\n| Performance | Image lazy loading, critical CSS inline |\n| Accessibility | WCAG 2.1 AA compliance (ARIA labels, keyboard nav) |\n\n### Tech Stack Recommendation:\n- **Frontend**: Next.js 14+ with App Router + Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui components\n- **Backend API**: Next.js API routes or standalone Express server using Prisma ORM  \n- **Database**: PostgreSQL via Supabase (or local PG for dev)\n- **Authentication**: NextAuth.js with Clerk/Email provider\n- **Image Optimization**: next/image component with AWS S3/cloudinary storage\n- **Form Handling**: React Hook Form + Zod validation\n\n---\n\n## 2. Architecture Design\n\n### Directory Structure:\n```\npractice-hackathon/coffee-shop/\n├── app/\n│   ├── (public)/          # Public routes (customers)\n│   │   ├── page.tsx       # Homepage\n│   │   ├── menu/\n│   │   │   └── page.tsx   # Menu listing + filters\n│   │   └── contact/page.tsx  # Contact form\n│   ├── admin/             # Protected routes (staff only)\n│   │   ├── dashboard/\n│   │   │   ├── orders/    # Order management table\n│   │   │   ├── menu-items/# CRUD operations per item\n│   │   └── analytics/page.tsx  # Charts/metrics views\n│   ├── api/               # Backend API endpoints (internal)\n│   ├── layout.tsx         # Root layout with providers\n│   └── globals.css        # Global styles + Tailwind\n├── components/            # Reusable React components\n│   ├── ui/                # shadcn/ui base components\n│   ├── hero-section/      # Homepage banner variants\n│   ├── menu-card.tsx      # Product card component with image badges\n│   └── contact-form/      # Form validation wrapper\n├── lib/                   # Utilities & configurations\n│   ├── db.ts              # Prisma client singleton\n│   ├── auth.ts            # NextAuth configuration\n│   ├── validations/       # Zod schema files (menu, orders)\n│   └── services/          # External API wrappers (payments if needed)\n├── prisma/                # Database layer\n│   ├── schema.prisma      # Full DB model definition\n│   └── migrations/        # Schema migration versions\n├── public/               # Static assets not handled by Next.js\n├── hooks.ts              # Custom React Hooks (useMenuData, useForm)\n└── types/                # TypeScript definitions (optional with TS inferrable types)\n\n```\n\n### Database Schema Design:\n\n**Core Tables:**\n\n1. **`MenuItem` Table**: `id`, name, description, price, category_id, image_url, is_active, created_at  \n   - Categories linked via foreign key for menu organization\n   - Boolean flags for availability/featured status\n\n2.  ```mermaid\n    **Customer** & Customer: id, email (unique), name, phone, preferences JSON\n  \n3. `Category` Table`: category, description\n     image_url, created_at\n     \n4.`OrderTable): id, table_number customer_id order_date total_amount status (pending/completed/cancelled)\n\n5. StaffProfile`, staff_id, role, permissions array\n  \n6-  **NotificationLog** & Notification: notification_type message sent_at recipient_type\n\n### API Endpoints List:\n| Method | Endpoint                     | Purpose                         | Notes              |\n|--------|-----------------------------|----------------------------------|--------------------|\n| GET    |/api/menu                   | Fetch all menu items            | Supports filtering by category/status                      |                                      ||     /menu/{id}                | Get single item with reviews  |                                    |                    POST      |/orders                     Create new order                  Customer + staff can create                                     ||         orders/:{id}/complete       Mark as completed        Requires auth admin role only                         GET    |/api/contact/form          | Get form field configs (optional customization             |                            ||     contact/submit            Submit inquiry           Validates with Zod schema                             \n\n---\n\n## 4. Phased Implementation Plan:\n### Phase 1 - Foundation & Public Routes\n**Goal**: Deploy a functioning customer-facing site without admin features\n\n- [ ] Initialize Next.js project with Tailwind CSS + App Router  \n- [ ] Set up PostgreSQL database + seed initial menu categories (Coffee, Hot Tea, Iced Drinks, Food) and sample items    \n- [ ] Build responsive homepage hero section (image banner, about blurb, featured items carousel), footer nav\n    - [ ] Create shared `MenuCard` component with image lazy loading via next/image, dietary badge icons overlay\n        - [ ] Implement MenuPage: category filter tabs, paginated results, SEO metadata dynamic from route params    \n-  **Contact form**: React Hook Form + Zod validation email field required submit handler sends POST to internal API returns success message\n\n---  \n### Phase 2 - Authentication & Protected Admin Routes:\n\n**Goal**: Secure routes for staff-only access with basic dashboard features\n\n- [ ] Configure NextAuth.js with environment-based provider (GitHub/Clerk for demo purposes)    \n    -  **Protected routes** via middleware.ts role checks using session user data\n        - [ ] Dashboard home page displays recent orders table with status badges, total sales KPI widget card   \n            - Menu item CRUD pages: individual edit form uses shadcn/ui inputs (text area select switch), image upload preview cloudinary integration if needed    DELETE /orders/:id endpoint marks cancelled or refunds order    \n-  **Analytics view**: Chart.js/Recharts rendering daily revenue trends, top-sold items bar chart\n        - Category performance pie/donut charts using server-side data fetching from database aggregations\n\n---  \n### Phase 3 - Advanced Features & Polish (Bonus if time permits):\n**Goal**: Add value-add features for real-world deployment scenarios  \n\n- [ ] Implement WhatsApp/Email notifications via Twilio/SendGrid mock services   \n    - QR code generation table ordering flow, pre-order system with pickup timestamp scheduling    \n        - Stripe payment intent integration test cards in sandbox mode  \n            - SEO optimizations: Dynamic open graph images per menu page category route group for different content previews\n                [ ] Write end-to-end Playwright tests critical user flows (home browsing order placement form submission admin login)  \n\n---\n\n## 5. Technical Notes & Decisions Made Ahead of Implementation Phase:\n\n**Not writing code yet**: User approved this plan - now entering build phase once they confirm readiness to proceed with actual implementation. All components will use shadcn/ui for consistency, semantic HTML elements where possible, responsive utilities via Tailwind arbitrary values (e.g., grid-cols-[12_4] aspect ratio math). Database migrations managed via Prisma Studio preview during dev environment setup using `npx prisma studio` before production deployment verification.\n\n---"}}],"usage":{"input_tokens":22698,"cache_creation_input_tokens":0,"cache_read_input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":1757,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"},"stop_reason":"tool_use","stop_details":null},"type":"assistant","uuid":"8ff3d302-fcfa-46f5-bf15-26c5c53fd467","timestamp":"2026-06-05T19:47:19.461Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/unnatigoel/FORGE_SPRINT_01/practice-hackathon","sessionId":"d44c80ee-5f69-4934-9ead-16766ad16e66","version":"2.1.165","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"serialized-weaving-cookie"}

{"parentUuid":"7f1c3bc9-0388-46c6-94e0-94ebff1d25b8","isSidechain":false,"message":{"id":"msg_2a112400b609beacfa14d611","type":"message","role":"assistant","model":"qwen3.5:9b","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Perfect! I've created your **comprehensive coffee shop web application architecture**. Here's the high-level breakdown:\n\n## Summary of What Was Designed ✅\n\n### 1. Requirements Analysis\n- Homepage (hero, featured items, about preview)\n- Menu page (categories, dietary badges, filters)  \n- Contact form (validations, success/error states)\n- Admin dashboard (orders, menu management, analytics)\n- Mobile responsive + SEO optimized per 2025 standards\n\n### 2. Architecture Design\n```\npractice-hackathon/coffee-shop/\n├── app/(public)/          # Customer routes: home, menu, contact  \n├── admin/dashboard/       # Protected staff-only pages\n├── components/ui/         # shadcn/ui reusable widgets\n├── lib/db.ts             # Prisma client singleton\n└── prisma/schema.prisma  # Database models + relations\n```\n\n### 3. Key Tech Decisions Made Ahead of Implementation:\n| Layer | Technology Choice | Why |\n|-------|-------------------|-----|\n| Framework | Next.js 14+ App Router | SEO, image optimization built-in |\n| Styling | Tailwind CSS | Mobile-first responsive breakpoints |\n| Database | PostgreSQL via Prisma ORM | Strongly typed relationships for menu/orders schema |\n\n### 4. API Endpoints Defined:\n- `GET /api/menu` - List items with filtering  \n- `POST /orders` - Customer order placement  \n- Admin routes protected by authentication middleware  \n\n---\n\n**Ready to proceed?** I have the complete plan written out in detail at `/Users/unnatigoel/.claude/plans/serialized-weaving-cookie.md`. \n\nShall I start building **Phase 1 (Foundation & Public Routes)** now?"}],"usage":{"input_tokens":24570,"cache_creation_input_tokens":0,"cache_read_input_tokens":0,"output_tokens":432,"server_tool_use":{"web_search_requests":0,"web_fetch_requests":0},"service_tier":"standard","cache_creation":{"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0},"inference_geo":"","iterations":[],"speed":"standard"},"stop_reason":"end_turn","stop_details":null},"type":"assistant","uuid":"9fc4591f-97b8-4e84-b42a-e77bf612577d","timestamp":"2026-06-05T19:48:27.807Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/unnatigoel/FORGE_SPRINT_01/practice-hackathon","sessionId":"d44c80ee-5f69-4934-9ead-16766ad16e66","version":"2.1.165","gitBranch":"HEAD","slug":"serialized-weaving-cookie"}

{"parentUuid":"24e6f09a-ce93-49dd-accf-75852e7c7827","isSidechain":false,"attachment":{"type":"skill_listing","content":"- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., \"what car to buy\" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.\n- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors (\"from now on when X\", \"each time X\", \"whenever X\", \"before/after X\") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions (\"allow X\", \"add permission\", \"move permission to\"), env vars (\"set X=Y\"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: \"allow npm commands\", \"add bq permission to global settings\", \"move permission to user settings\", \"set DEBUG=true\", \"when claude stops show X\". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.\n- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: \"rebind ctrl+s\", \"add a chord shortcut\", \"change the submit key\", \"customize keybindings\".\n- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.\n- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.\n- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.\n- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.\n- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. \"check the deploy every 5 minutes\", \"keep running /babysit-prs\"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.\n- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.\nTRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it \"looks like a one-liner\" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).\nSKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).\n- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).\n- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation\n- review: Review a pull request\n- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch","skillCount":13,"isInitial":true,"names":["deep-research","update-config","keybindings-help","verify","code-review","simplify","fewer-permission-prompts","loop","claude-api","run","init","review","security-review"]},"type":"attachment","uuid":"ec125c68-7856-434d-ac96-7d5fa8c3306e","timestamp":"2026-06-05T17:54:23.242Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/unnatigoel/FORGE_SPRINT_01","sessionId":"d69bbb71-523e-4e2f-85f8-dfd59c0e2e1e","version":"2.1.165","gitBranch":"HEAD"}

{"parentUuid":"1b781a7d-b975-4900-9c4e-7cf3af7f7e4e","isSidechain":false,"promptId":"64f7b2b4-8c44-486b-a63f-f3ca09496257","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Please analyze this codebase and create a CLAUDE.md file, which will be given to future instances of Claude Code to operate in this repository.\n\nWhat to add:\n1. Commands that will be commonly used, such as how to build, lint, and run tests. Include the necessary commands to develop in this codebase, such as how to run a single test.\n2. High-level code architecture and structure so that future instances can be productive more quickly. Focus on the \"big picture\" architecture that requires reading multiple files to understand.\n\nUsage notes:\n- If there's already a CLAUDE.md, suggest improvements to it.\n- When you make the initial CLAUDE.md, do not repeat yourself and do not include obvious instructions like \"Provide helpful error messages to users\", \"Write unit tests for all new utilities\", \"Never include sensitive information (API keys, tokens) in code or commits\".\n- Avoid listing every component or file structure that can be easily discovered.\n- Don't include generic development practices.\n- If there are Cursor rules (in .cursor/rules/ or .cursorrules) or Copilot rules (in .github/copilot-instructions.md), make sure to include the important parts.\n- If there is a README.md, make sure to include the important parts.\n- Do not make up information such as \"Common Development Tasks\", \"Tips for Development\", \"Support and Documentation\" unless this is expressly included in other files that you read.\n- Be sure to prefix the file with the following text:\n\n```\n# CLAUDE.md\n\nThis file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.\n```"}]},"isMeta":true,"uuid":"8136a61d-813a-4691-9503-0b8d388b1ae0","timestamp":"2026-06-05T17:57:21.842Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/unnatigoel/FORGE_SPRINT_01","sessionId":"d69bbb71-523e-4e2f-85f8-dfd59c0e2e1e","version":"2.1.165","gitBranch":"HEAD"}

{"parentUuid":"b584963f-e6de-41dd-9503-dce8cb51e47c","isSidechain":false,"promptId":"e82f8804-eba9-4fa6-9376-5f924c1c17e1","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":"<command-message>claude-api</command-message>\n<command-name>/claude-api</command-name>"},"uuid":"7b32d7f2-0436-4f15-9800-150e9169ee23","timestamp":"2026-06-05T18:01:42.273Z","userType":"external","entrypoint":"cli","cwd":"/Users/unnatigoel/FORGE_SPRINT_01","sessionId":"d69bbb71-523e-4e2f-85f8-dfd59c0e2e1e","version":"2.1.165","gitBranch":"HEAD"}
{"parentUuid":"7b32d7f2-0436-4f15-9800-150e9169ee23","isSidechain":false,"promptId":"e82f8804-eba9-4fa6-9376-5f924c1c17e1","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Base directory for this skill: /private/tmp/claude-501/bundled-skills/2.1.165/cfef1382b184ba47b58c1d0471969788/claude-api\n\n# Building LLM-Powered Applications with Claude\n\nThis skill helps you build LLM-powered applications with Claude. Choose the right surface based on your needs, detect the project language, then read the relevant language-specific documentation.\n\n## Before You Start\n\nScan the target file (or, if no target file, the prompt and project) for non-Anthropic provider markers — `import openai`, `from openai`, `langchain_openai`, `OpenAI(`, `gpt-4`, `gpt-5`, file names like `agent-openai.py` or `*-generic.py`, or any explicit instruction to keep the code provider-neutral. If you find any, stop and tell the user that this skill produces Claude/Anthropic SDK code; ask whether they want to switch the file to Claude or want a non-Claude implementation. Do not edit a non-Anthropic file with Anthropic SDK calls.\n\n## Output Requirement\n\nWhen the user asks you to add, modify, or implement a Claude feature, your code must call Claude through one of:\n\n1. **The official Anthropic SDK** for the project's language (`anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai/sdk`, `com.anthropic.*`, etc.). This is the default whenever a supported SDK exists for the project.\n2. **Raw HTTP** (`curl`, `requests`, `fetch`, `httpx`, etc.) — only when the user explicitly asks for cURL/REST/raw HTTP, the project is a shell/cURL project, or the language has no official SDK.\n\nNever mix the two — don't reach for `requests`/`fetch` in a Python or TypeScript project just because it feels lighter. Never fall back to OpenAI-compatible shims.\n\n**Never guess SDK usage.** Function names, class names, namespaces, method signatures, and import paths must come from explicit documentation — either the `{lang}/` files in this skill or the official SDK repositories or documentation links listed in `shared/live-sources.md`. If the binding you need is not explicitly documented in the skill files, WebFetch the relevant SDK repo from `shared/live-sources.md` before writing code. Do not infer Ruby/Java/Go/PHP/C# APIs from cURL shapes or from another language's SDK.\n\n## Defaults\n\nUnless the user requests otherwise:\n\nFor the Claude model version, please use Claude Opus 4.8, which you can access via the exact model string `claude-opus-4-8`. Please default to using adaptive thinking (`thinking: {type: \"adaptive\"}`) for anything remotely complicated. And finally, please default to streaming for any request that may involve long input, long output, or high `max_tokens` — it prevents hitting request timeouts. Use the SDK's `.get_final_message()` / `.finalMessage()` helper to get the complete response if you don't need to handle individual stream events\n\n---\n\n## Subcommands\n\nIf the User Request at the bottom of this prompt is a bare subcommand string (no prose), search every **Subcommands** table in this document — including any in sections appended below — and follow the matching Action column directly. This lets users invoke specific flows via `/claude-api <subcommand>`. If no table in the document matches, treat the request as normal prose.\n\n| Subcommand | Action |\n|---|---|\n| `migrate` | Migrate existing Claude API code to a newer model. **Read `shared/model-migration.md` immediately** and follow it in order: Step 0 (confirm scope — ask which files/directories before any edit), Step 1 (classify each file), then the per-target breaking-changes section. Do not summarize the guide — execute it. If the user did not name a target model, ask which model to migrate to in the same turn as the scope question. |\n\n---\n\n## Language Detection\n\nBefore reading code examples, determine which language the user is working in:\n\n1. **Look at project files** to infer the language:\n\n   - `*.py`, `requirements.txt`, `pyproject.toml`, `setup.py`, `Pipfile` → **Python** — read from `python/`\n   - `*.ts`, `*.tsx`, `package.json`, `tsconfig.json` → **TypeScript** — read from `typescript/`\n   - `*.js`, `*.jsx` (no `.ts` files present) → **TypeScript** — JS uses the same SDK, read from `typescript/`\n   - `*.java`, `pom.xml`, `build.gradle` → **Java** — read from `java/`\n   - `*.kt`, `*.kts`, `build.gradle.kts` → **Java** — Kotlin uses the Java SDK, read from `java/`\n   - `*.scala`, `build.sbt` → **Java** — Scala uses the Java SDK, read from `java/`\n   - `*.go`, `go.mod` → **Go** — read from `go/`\n   - `*.rb`, `Gemfile` → **Ruby** — read from `ruby/`\n   - `*.cs`, `*.csproj` → **C#** — read from `csharp/`\n   - `*.php`, `composer.json` → **PHP** — read from `php/`\n\n2. **If multiple languages detected** (e.g., both Python and TypeScript files):\n\n   - Check which language the user's current file or question relates to\n   - If still ambiguous, ask: \"I detected both Python and TypeScript files. Which language are you using for the Claude API integration?\"\n\n3. **If language can't be inferred** (empty project, no source files, or unsupported language):\n\n   - Use AskUserQuestion with options: Python, TypeScript, Java, Go, Ruby, cURL/raw HTTP, C#, PHP\n   - If AskUserQuestion is unavailable, default to Python examples and note: \"Showing Python examples. Let me know if you need a different language.\"\n\n4. **If unsupported language detected** (Rust, Swift, C++, Elixir, etc.):\n\n   - Suggest cURL/raw HTTP examples from `curl/` and note that community SDKs may exist\n   - Offer to show Python or TypeScript examples as reference implementations\n\n5. **If user needs cURL/raw HTTP examples**, read from `curl/`.\n\n### Language-Specific Feature Support\n\n| Language   | Tool Runner | Managed Agents | Notes                                 |\n| ---------- | ----------- | -------------- | ------------------------------------- |\n| Python     | Yes (beta)  | Yes (beta)     | Full support — `@beta_tool` decorator |\n| TypeScript | Yes (beta)  | Yes (beta)     | Full support — `betaZodTool` + Zod    |\n| Java       | Yes (beta)  | Yes (beta)     | Beta tool use with annotated classes  |\n| Go         | Yes (beta)  | Yes (beta)     | `BetaToolRunner` in `toolrunner` pkg  |\n| Ruby       | Yes (beta)  | Yes (beta)     | `BaseTool` + `tool_runner` in beta    |\n| C#         | Yes (beta)  | Yes (beta)     | `BetaToolRunner` + raw JSON schema    |\n| PHP        | Yes (beta)  | Yes (beta)     | `BetaRunnableTool` + `toolRunner()`   |\n| cURL       | N/A         | Yes (beta)     | Raw HTTP, no SDK features             |\n\n> **Managed Agents code examples**: dedicated language-specific READMEs are provided for Python, TypeScript, Go, Ruby, PHP, Java, and cURL (`{lang}/managed-agents/README.md`, `curl/managed-agents.md`). Read your language's README plus the language-agnostic `shared/managed-agents-*.md` concept files. **Agents are persistent — create once, reference by ID.** Store the agent ID returned by `agents.create` and pass it to every subsequent `sessions.create`; do not call `agents.create` in the request path. The Anthropic CLI (`ant`) is one convenient way to create agents and environments from version-controlled YAML — see `shared/anthropic-cli.md`. If a binding you need isn't shown in the README, WebFetch the relevant entry from `shared/live-sources.md` rather than guess. C# has beta Managed Agents support via `client.Beta.Agents` and related namespaces.\n\n---\n\n## Which Surface Should I Use?\n\n> **Start simple.** Default to the simplest tier that meets your needs. Single API calls and workflows handle most use cases — only reach for agents when the task genuinely requires open-ended, model-driven exploration.\n\n| Use Case                                        | Tier            | Recommended Surface       | Why                                                          |\n| ----------------------------------------------- | --------------- | ------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ |\n| Classification, summarization, extraction, Q&A  | Single LLM call | **Claude API**            | One request, one response                                    |\n| Batch processing or embeddings                  | Single LLM call | **Claude API**            | Specialized endpoints                                        |\n| Multi-step pipelines with code-controlled logic | Workflow        | **Claude API + tool use** | You orchestrate the loop                                     |\n| Custom agent with your own tools                | Agent           | **Claude API + tool use** | Maximum flexibility                                          |\n| Server-managed stateful agent with workspace    | Agent           | **Managed Agents**        | Anthropic runs the loop and hosts the tool-execution sandbox |\n| Persisted, versioned agent configs              | Agent           | **Managed Agents**        | Agents are stored objects; sessions pin to a version         |\n| Long-running multi-turn agent with file mounts  | Agent           | **Managed Agents**        | Per-session containers, SSE event stream, Skills + MCP       |\n\n> **Note:** Managed Agents is the right choice when you want Anthropic to run the agent loop *and* host the container where tools execute — file ops, bash, code execution all run in the per-session workspace. If you want to host the compute yourself or run your own custom tool runtime, Claude API + tool use is the right choice — use the tool runner for automatic loop handling, or the manual loop for fine-grained control (approval gates, custom logging, conditional execution).\n\n> **Cloud-provider access.** **Claude Platform on AWS** is Anthropic-operated with same-day API parity — Managed Agents and every feature in this skill work there, **except self-hosted sandboxes** (see `shared/claude-platform-on-aws.md`). **Amazon Bedrock**, **Google Vertex AI**, and **Microsoft Foundry** do **not** support Managed Agents or Anthropic server-side tools; use **Claude API + tool use** on those.\n\n### Decision Tree\n\n```\nWhat does your application need?\n\n0. Which provider?\n   ├── First-party API or Claude Platform on AWS → continue (full surface available).\n   └── Amazon Bedrock, Google Vertex AI, or Microsoft Foundry → Claude API (+ tool use for agents); Managed Agents not available there.\n\n1. Single LLM call (classification, summarization, extraction, Q&A)\n   └── Claude API — one request, one response\n\n2. Do you want Anthropic to run the agent loop and host a per-session\n   container where Claude executes tools (bash, file ops, code)?\n   └── Yes → Managed Agents — server-managed sessions, persisted agent configs,\n       SSE event stream, Skills + MCP, file mounts.\n       Examples: \"stateful coding agent with a workspace per task\",\n                 \"long-running research agent that streams events to a UI\",\n                 \"agent with persisted, versioned config used across many sessions\"\n\n3. Workflow (multi-step, code-orchestrated, with your own tools)\n   └── Claude API with tool use — you control the loop\n\n4. Open-ended agent (model decides its own trajectory, your own tools, you host the compute)\n   └── Claude API agentic loop (maximum flexibility)\n```\n\n### Should I Build an Agent?\n\nBefore choosing the agent tier, check all four criteria:\n\n- **Complexity** — Is the task multi-step and hard to fully specify in advance?
